
Global renewable energy capacity grew fastest pace recorded last 20 years 2023, put world within reach meeting key climate target end decade, according International Energy Agency (IEA).
Additions world' renewable energy grew 50% last 510 gigawatts (GW) 2023, 22nd row renewable capacity additions set new record, according figures IEA.
"spectacular" growth offers "real chance" global governments meeting pledge agreed Cop28 climate talks November triple renewable energy capacity 2030 significantly reduce consumption fossil fuels, IEA added.
IEA' latest report found solar power accounted three-quarters new renewable energy capacity installed worldwide last. Most world' new solar power built China, installed solar power last entire world commissioned before, despite cutting subsidies 2020 2021.
Record rates growth across Europe, US Brazil put renewables track overtake coal largest source global electricity generation early 2025, IEA said. 2028, forecasts renewable energy sources account 42% global electricity generation.
Tripling global renewable energy end decade help cut carbon emissions one five main climate targets designed prevent runaway global heating, alongside doubling energy efficiency, cutting methane emissions, transitioning away fossil fuels, scaling financing emerging developing economies.
Fatih Birol, IEA' executive director, said: "It' excellent news see historical spectacular growth renewable energy." added while report shows global renewable capacity already course increase two---half times 2030, yet expected reach Cop28 goal tripling renewables.
" there, million miles away," Birol said. " governments tools needed close gap."
Birol downplayed concerns fast-rising costs facing wind developers US Europe drag industry' long-term growth. combination higher interest rates supply chain costs forced some developers cancel big offshore wind projects, raised concerns future technology.??????????
"I would very careful confuse cyclical increase costs structural increase costs. Costs continue come down industry, cyclical costs today begin ease," Birol said.
Emerging developing economies face challenges accessing finance, strengthening governance creating robust regulatory frameworks essential reducing investor risk attract investment, IEA said, may include establishing new targets policies countries where they do exist yet.
" me, most important challenge international community rapidly scaling financing deployment renewables most emerging developing economies, many being left behind new energy economy. Success meeting tripling goal hinge," Birol said.

Wind turbines solar panels overtaken fossil fuels generate 30% European Union' electricity first half, report found.
Power generation burning coal, oil gas fell 17% first six months 2024 compared same period before, according climate thinktank Ember. found continued shift away polluting fuels led one-third drop sector' emissions since first half 2022.
Chris Rosslowe, analyst Ember, said rise wind solar narrowing role fossil fuels. " witnessing historic shift power sector, happening rapidly."
report found EU power plants burned 24% less coal 14% less gas first half 2023 first half 2024. shift comes despite small uptick electricity demand followed two years decline linked pandemic Ukraine war.
"If member states can keep momentum wind solar deployment then freedom fossil power reliance truly start come into view," said Rosslowe.
Europe among biggest historical polluters contributed planet-heating gas made extreme weather violent, some most ambitious targets clean economy. Since Russia' invasion Ukraine, European leaders sped shift renewables stronger rhetoric looser permitting rules.
while solar power boomed, wind industry struggled high inflation top continued opposition politicians public. EU installed record 16.2GW new wind power capacity 2023, according lobby group Wind Power Europe, half what needed meet climate targets end decade.
Scenarios modelled Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change (IPCC) International Energy Agency show most electricity needed power clean economy come rays sunlight shining panels gusts wind spinning turbines.
Ember report found 13 member states generated electricity wind solar fossil fuels first half. Germany, Belgium, Hungary Netherlands hit milestone first time, authors found.
Andrea Hahmann, scientist Denmark Technical University who co-wrote IPCC report chapter energy systems, said development "significant surprising".
"Strong winds prevalent during first six months 2024 northern Europe, where most wind energy generated," she said. " 'crossing lines' demonstrates EU' electricity transition possible, should give pessimism. renewable energy targets must met substantial achievable proper policy measures."
article' headline updated 30 July 2024 make clear wind solar energy make 30% EU electricity. other renewables such hydropower included, proportion would 50%. graphics updated make clear source data came whole EU, rather just 13 member states.

China expected account almost 60% all renewable energy capacity installed worldwide between now 2030, according International Energy Agency.

found world' renewable energy capacity course outpace 2030 goals set governments roughly equal power systems China, EU, India US combined.

growth solar capacity 2030 account 80% all new renewable power added globally end decade, according IEA. expects rollout accelerate due declining costs policy support enable homes businesses invest solar panels reduce electricity bills.

green energy boom means renewables track grow 2.7 times 2030, exceeding goals set out governments nearly 25%, report found. growth still falls narrowly short commitment made world leaders triple renewables end decade cut world' reliance fossil fuels.

First, global leaders need recognise "critical importance building power grids" speed connection new renewable energy projects, said. IEA found least 1,650 gigawatts renewable capacity now advanced stages development waiting grid connection, 150GW higher point last.

amount wind solar power under construction China now nearly twice much rest world combined, report found.
Research published Thursday Global Energy Monitor (GEM), NGO, found China 180 gigawatts (GW) utility-scale solar power under construction 159GW wind power. brings total wind solar power under construction 339GW, well ahead 40GW under construction US.
researchers only looked solar farms capacity 20MW or, feed directly into grid. means total volume solar power China much higher, small scale solar farms account 40% China' solar capacity.
findings underscore China' leading position global renewable energy production time when US increasingly worried Chinese overcapacity dumping, particularly solar industry.
China experienced boom renewables recent years, encouraged strong government support. Xi Jinping, China' president, stressed need "new quality productive forces", slogan signifies desire pivot China' economy towards technology innovation. Xi said "new quality productive forces" includes strengthening green manufacturing.
Between March 2023 March 2024, China installed solar had previous three years combined, rest world combined 2023, GEM analysts found. China track reach 1,200GW installed wind solar capacity end 2024, six years ahead government' target.
" unabated wave construction guarantees China continue leading wind solar installation near future, far ahead rest world," report said.
However, analysts cautioned still renewable capacity needed if China meet target reducing carbon intensity economy 18%, important factor reducing emissions. Carbon intensity refers how many grams CO2 released produce kilowatt hour electricity.
Earlier analysis suggests China need install between 1,600GW 1,800GW wind solar energy 2030 meet target producing 25% all energy non-fossil sources.
Between 2020 2023, only 30% growth energy consumption met renewable sources, compared target 50%.
" obviously important China keep adding renewable energy meet targets," said Li Shuo, director China Climate Hub Asia Policy Institute Washington DC. " it' simple you just keep building solved... [because] there no sign country trying steer away coal consumption."
Previous analysis GEM Centre Research Energy Clean Air, thinktank, found approvals new coal power plants increased fourfold 2022-2023, compared previous five- period 2016-2020, despite pledge 2021 "strictly control" new coal power. Growth total coal consumption increased average 0.5% 3.8% between two time periods.
Geopolitical tensions such war Ukraine, focused many countries' attention energy supplies, major power cuts parts China recent years, increased Chinese officials' concerns energy security.
China' power grid remains reliant coal, officials see necessary mitigate intermittency renewable energy. officials often see coal industry being safe way boost local GDP figures, although clean energy sectors now biggest driver China' economic growth, accounting 40% GDP expansion 2023.
Analysts say better storage grid flexibility necessary efficiently use increasing volume clean energy being generated China' wind solar farms.
Chinese government aware challenge, naming lithium-ion batteries one "new three" technologies important creating high-quality growth, along electric vehicles solar panels. Last, $11bn invested grid-connected batteries, increase 364% 2022.
GEM report highlighted China' lead actually building planned renewable energy infrastructure. 339GW wind solar reached construction stage represents one-third proposed projects, far surpassing global construction rate 7%.
"China' renewable energy pipeline two times larger rest world," Li said. " question should increasingly ask ourselves, how come rest world so slow?"

latest government figures reveal UK' renewable capacity fallen average increase 4.45% past three years, compared average 9.67% annual increase globally.
analysis follows government' announcement approve 100 new oil gas licences.

rest world recorded much higher levels growth renewable capacity compared UK last three years. 2020, renewable capacity grew 10.3%, followed 9.1% 2021 9.6% 2022. averages 9.67% increase, double UK same period.

Fossil fuel companies running " massive mis- disinformation campaign" so countries slow down adoption renewable energy speed they "transition away" carbon-intensive economy, UN said.
Selwin Hart, assistant secretary general UN, said talk global "backlash" against climate action being stoked fossil fuel industry, effort persuade world leaders delay emissions-cutting policies. perception among many political observers rejection climate policies result campaign, rather reflecting reality what people think, added.
"There prevailing narrative - lot being pushed fossil fuel industry enablers - climate action too difficult, it' too expensive," said. " absolutely critical leaders, all us, push back explain people value climate action, consequences climate inaction."
contrasted perception backlash findings biggest poll ever conducted climate, found clear majorities people around world supporting measures reduce greenhouse gas emissions. survey found 72% people wanted "quick transition" away fossil fuels, including majorities countries produce most coal, oil gas. Green parties plans may suffered reverses some parts world, said, others they gained seats, seen policies would once considered radical enter mainstream.
Governments must take note, said Hart, who acts special adviser climate UN secretary general, António Guterres. " should alert political leaders - those ambitious only right side history, they're side people well.
"Climate appears dropping down list priorities leaders," said. " really need leaders now deliver maximum ambition. need maximum cooperation. Unfortunately, seeing moment."
Paris agreement, reached Cop21 2015, needs enhanced requirement new national plans emissions reduction, UN says. Photograph: Ian Langsdon/EPA
warned consequences inaction being felt rich countries well poor. US, many thousands people finding increasingly impossible insure homes, extreme weather worsens. " directly due climate crisis, directly due use fossil fuels," said. "Ordinary people having pay price climate crisis while fossil fuel industry continues reap excess profits still receives massive government subsidies."
Yet world never better equipped tackle climate breakdown, Hart added. "Renewables cheapest they've ever, pace energy transition accelerating," said.
Governments should take care ensure climate policies did place unfair burdens those low incomes, poorly designed measures hurt poor, according Hart. "Each country really need ensure transition well planned minimise impact people vulnerable populations, because lot so-called pushback comes when there' perception costs poor vulnerable persons being disproportionately felt," said.
reason, UN calling new national plans emissions reductions required under 2015 Paris agreement, governments must set out clearly just targets how they achieved through policy, what probable impacts.
new national plans, called nationally determined contributions (NDCs), should " consultative possible so whole segments society - young people, women, children, workers - able provide perspective how transition should planned well-managed, how financed", said.
"Despite everything see [ form extreme weather], still seeing level ambition or action world desperately needs."

report, Green Energy Markets, said NSW' legislated electricity infrastructure roadmap indicates state would need generate 33,600 gigawatt hours renewable energy projects place end 2029.
score, NSW less halfway goal forecast output projects already committed or contracted 12,911GWh end 2023. Those projects amounted just 6GW capacity, leaving state almost 7.5GW wind solar farms needed hit target.

Still, biggest electricity generator, NSW " chokepoint" national transition off fossil fuels, said Tristan Edis, senior Green Energy Markets analyst author report. time planners " take foot off pedal", said.
NSW largely dodged major strains power grid summer heatwaves main population centres, including Sydney, mostly limited single-day events.
state' challenges may worsen near term. Australia' largest power station - 2.88GW Eraring coal-fired plant - slated shut August 2025. Talks extension continue between NSW owner Origin, exit remove quarter NSW' so-called baseload generation capacity.

His estimate, though, odds Clean Energy Investment Group report found solar farms past five years taking 705 days average secure approval, battery projects 540 days windfarms almost 3,500 days.
senior executive renewables industry, who requested anonymity, said NSW worst among states territories planning approvals.
executive cited changes conditions once approvals had granted deter future investment. These included draft wind energy guidelines trigger retrospective alterations, even within state' renewable energy zones originally set streamline developments.
case Central West-Orana zone, initial target amount power would excess transmission capacity put 0.3% December 2021. 2022, however, target curtailment had lifted 14-fold 4.37%, even before limits outside zone, such Sydney, added.

prospects world staying within 1.5C limit global heating brightened owing "staggering" growth renewable energy green investment past two years, chief world' energy watchdog said.
Fatih Birol, executive director International Energy Agency, world' foremost energy economist, said much needed done rapid uptake solar power electric vehicles encouraging.
"Despite scale challenges, I feel optimistic I felt two years ago," said interview. "Solar photovoltaic installations electric vehicle sales perfectly line what said they should, track reach net zero 2050, thus stay within 1.5C. Clean energy investments last two years seen staggering 40% increase."
Birol noted greenhouse gas emissions energy sector "still stubbornly high", extreme weather seen around world had shown climate already changing " frightening speed".
IEA, report entitled Net Zero Roadmap, published Tuesday morning, called developed countries 2050 net zero targets, including UK, bring them forward several years.
report found "almost all countries must move forward targeted net zero dates", most developed countries 2050. Some developed countries earlier dates, such Germany 2045 Austria Iceland 2040 many developing countries they much later, 2060 case China 2070 India.
Cop28, UN climate summit held Dubai November December, offered key opportunity countries set out tougher emissions-cutting plans, Birol said.
wants see Cop28 agree tripling renewable energy 2030, 75% cut methane energy sector same date. latter achieved little cost, because high gas prices mean plugging leaks oil gas wells can profitable.
Birol warned geopolitical situation, many nations loggerheads war Ukraine, still frosty relations between US China, would make difficult summit.
said: " most important challenge [ limiting temperature rises 1.5C above pre-industrial levels] lack international cooperation. Cop28 critical juncture, should send strong signal energy markets governments taking climate seriously. They should move reduce consumption unabated fossil fuels."
called Cop28 agree doubling energy efficiency. " reduce fossil fuel emissions, need reduce demand fossil fuels. golden condition, if reach our climate goals," said.
Birol stopped short endorsing call some countries made full phase-out fossil fuels 2050 agreed Cop28, said all countries must work reducing fossil fuel use.
Rishi Sunak, UK' prime minister, last week reaffirmed his commitment net zero 2050, reversed delayed some key policies would help attain goal. Sunak planning large new round North Sea oil gas licences, despite clear advice IEA no new upstream oil or gas projects should built road net zero.
Birol refused single out any countries, made clear richest countries should moving much faster renewable energy, energy efficiency, reducing fossil fuel use bringing forward net zero targets.
Birol said: "Advanced economies special responsibilities fighting climate change. What I would expect advanced economies do increase ambition further, rather reducing. Clean energy would create lots new jobs employment, build modern industry [needed] competitive position other countries. Industry knows next chapter global industry based clean energy technologies."
Tessa Khan, executive director Uplift, campaigning organisation, said: " [IEA report] yet confirmation world' energy experts can't new oil gas projects if going stay within safe climate, massively scaling renewables key achieving.
"Yet UK part tiny club wealthy countries while professing lead climate massively expanding oil gas production. Just five nations - US, Canada, Australia, Norway UK - responsible half all planned oil gas field developments now 2050."
oil industry fought against government support clean technologies half century, Guardian can reveal, even vast subsidies propped polluting business model.
lobbied lawmakers block support low-carbon technologies such solar panels, electric cars heat pumps far back 1960s, analysis shows. Trade associations US Europe stymied green innovations under guise supporting "technology neutral" approach avoiding damage done burning fuels.
same incumbents happy lobby government support when they getting started, had continued benefit since, said Dario Kenner, visiting research fellow University Sussex who trawled through decades public statements American Petroleum Institute (API) FuelsEurope.
"It' obviously hypocritical call technological neutrality when you dominant technology," said.
Kenner documented dozens examples oil industry pressuring governments hold back support renewable energy, restrict funding development clean technologies weaken environmental rules favoured uptake.
Lobbyists both sides Atlantic argued government subsidies clean technologies distorted free markets. Activists say position "dishonest" because oil industry benefits tax credits other financial help governments, pays only fraction damage fuels do people planet.
2022, total subsidies fossil fuels - including costs society - came $760bn (£592bn) US $310bn (£264bn) EU, according International Monetary Fund.
revelations "outrageous frankly unsurprising", said Shira Stanton campaign group Beyond Fossil Fuels, who involved analysis.
" proven fossil fuel industry caused climate crisis deliberately lied they hid science," she said. "So finding out they knew renewable energy such threat industry they had lobby policymakers rig market against cleaner cheaper technology protect profits just par course."
Some interventions may slowed growth technologies scientists say key stopping planet heating.
1975, after global oil crisis, API opposed energy saving bill included refundable income tax credits heat pumps homes.
" United States large resource base conventional energy such oil, gas coal," said. "Expeditious development these supplies can make significant contribution only improving US energy independence, create healthy economy."
Some industry' early efforts hold back competition later helped argue society do without.
1967, API protested against bill promote development electric cars argument governments should "stimulate all efforts industry eliminate automotive pollution, rather dedicate federal funds promotion any single possible solution".
half century later, 2005, lobbyists fought bill support electric cars argument they developed enough.
" United States, world, cannot afford leave age oil before realistic alternatives fully place," said Red Cavaney, then president API. " important remember man left stone age because ran out stones - leave age oil because ran out oil."
electric car charging point London. Lobbyists both sides Atlantic argued government subsidies clean technologies distorted free markets. Photograph: Zeynep Demir Aslim/Alamy
FuelsEurope, fought support electric vehicles decade, pushed weaken EU fuel efficiency standards 2017 so they would allow combustion engine cars burning alternative fuels. Critics say low-carbon fuels wants power cars expensive, inefficient such short supply they would better used planes ships, harder run electricity.
oil industry had moved denying climate change derailing climate action, said Anna Krajinska, vehicles analyst campaign group Transport Environment, involved analysis.
" push tech neutrality - particularly 'carbon neutral' fuels road transport - disingenuous attempt keep combustion engines burning fossil fuels," she said.
Some world' biggest oil companies invested clean energy projects they come under increasing pressure activists, investors governments. Armed big wallets skilled engineers, they argued they can lead transition carbon-neutral economy.
report International Energy Agency (IEA) November found oil gas companies accounted just 1% clean energy investments. described sector "marginal force best" transition.
Kenner said "ludicrous" debate individual oil company' transition plans when industry had spent so long fighting clean alternatives threaten market share.
" part trade associations lobby groups, they deliberately trying undermine same technologies people want them invest," said.
IEA report found oil gas producers would spend 20 times capital clean energy - rising 2.5% 2022 50% 2030 - line Paris agreement goal keeping planet heating 1.5C (2.7F) above pre-industrial levels end century.
Christina Figueres, Costa Rican diplomat architect agreement, told Guardian before Cop28 climate summit November she used believe industry needed seat table had lost hope after seeing use windfall profits since war Ukraine enrich shareholders - instead reinvesting them clean energy.
API FuelsEurope said they working reduce emissions.
API said: "America' natural gas oil industry working address risks climate change build lower-carbon future, while simultaneously meeting world' growing energy needs. Our members continue make significant progress reducing greenhouse gas emissions across operations, while leading development low-carbon solutions like carbon capture storage hydrogen critical meeting world' greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets."
FuelsEurope said: "FuelsEurope can state our industry transforming, developed comprehensive pathway how, together our partners, can contribute meeting 2050 climate neutrality challenge. 2050, latest, every litre liquid fuel transport net climate neutral, enabling so, together all existing proven sustainable alternative technologies, decarbonisation aviation, maritime road transport, refinery products supplying industrial value chain net zero CO2."
Kenner compared industry' lobbying gas lighting companies who fought arrival electric street lights, canal companies who protested against new railways. fighting off support emerging competitors, industries slowed transition new technologies.
" know history technological change it' often companies new technologies push forward - it' usually incumbents," said Kenner. "Blockbuster video going get Netflix online streaming."

G20 group developed developing economies, multilateral development banks they fund, put $142bn (£112bn) into fossil fuel developments overseas 2020 2022, according estimates compiled campaigning groups Oil Change International (OCI) Friends Earth US.
Canada, Japan South Korea biggest sources such finance three years studied, gas received funding either coal or oil.
G7 group biggest economies, Japan Canada belong, pledged 2022 halt overseas funding fossil fuels. while funding coal rapidly diminished, finance oil gas projects continued strong pace.
Some money going other developed economies, including Australia, much developing world. However, richer middle income countries still receive finance poorest.
most recent G7 pledge, study, phase out all overseas fossil fuel funding end 2022. OCI study concentrates period beginning fiscal 2020-21 each country, end fiscal 2022-23.
However, researchers found Japan had continued make new fossil fuel investments overseas past few weeks, mid-March 2024, exploiting loopholes promise end fossil fuel funding.
World Bank provided $1.2bn fossil fuels three- period, two-thirds went gas projects.
US, Germany Italy provided billions funding overseas fossil fuel projects before end 2022-23, according report published Tuesday. UK supplied $600m average.
Canada supplied just under $11bn average, 2020-22 period studied, while South Korea put forward $10bn Japan $7bn.
same three- period, G20 economies put $104bn into clean energy developments overseas, according report.
Claire O'Manique, public finance analyst OCI, said: "While rich countries continue drag feet claim they can't afford fund globally just energy transition, countries like Canada, Korea, Japan US appear no shortage public funds climate-wrecking fossil fuels.
" must continue hold wealthy countries accountable role funding climate crisis, demand they move first fastest fossil fuel phase-out, stop funding fossil fuels, they pay fair share globally just transition, loss damage adaptation finance."

Arima said: "Japan derailing transition renewable energy across Asia globally. Renewable energy accounted 30% world' electricity first time last following rapid rise wind solar power, according new figures.
report global power system found world may brink driving down fossil fuel generation, even overall demand electricity continues rise.
Clean electricity already helped slow growth fossil fuels almost two-thirds past 10 years, according report climate thinktank Ember. found renewables grown 19% electricity 2000 30% global electricity last.
" renewables future arrived," said Dave Jones, Ember' director global insights. "Solar, particular, accelerating faster anyone thought possible."
Solar main supplier electricity growth, according Ember, adding twice much new electricity generation coal 2023.
fastest-growing source electricity 19th consecutive, became largest source new electricity second running, after surpassing wind power.
first comprehensive review global electricity data covers 80 countries, represent 92% world' electricity demand, well historic data 215 countries.
surge clean electricity expected power 2% decrease global fossil fuel generation ahead, according Ember.
" decline power sector emissions now inevitable," said Jones. "2023 likely pivot point - peak emissions power sector - major turning point history energy. pace emissions falls depends how fast renewables revolution continues."
Although fossil fuel use world' electricity system may begin fall, continues play outsized role global energy - transport fuels, heavy industry heating.
separate study Energy Institute found last fossil fuels including oil, gas coal made 82% world' primary energy.
World leaders aiming grow renewables 60% global electricity 2030 under agreement struck UN' Cop28 climate change conference December.
would require countries triple current renewable electricity capacity next six years, would almost halve power sector emissions.


Faced worsening climate-driven disasters electricity grid increasingly supplied intermittent renewables, US rapidly installing huge batteries already starting help prevent power blackouts.
barely anything just few years ago, US now adding utility-scale batteries dizzying pace, having installed 20 gigawatts battery capacity electric grid, 5GW occurring just first seven months, according federal Energy Information Administration (EIA).
means battery storage equivalent output 20 nuclear reactors bolted America' electric grids barely four years, EIA predicting capacity double again 40GW 2025 if further planned expansions occur.
California Texas, both saw all-time highs battery-discharged grid power month, leading way growth, hulking batteries helping manage large amount clean yet intermittent solar wind energy these states added recent years.

explosion battery deployment even helped keep lights California summer, when previous years state seen electricity rationing or blackouts during intense heatwaves see air conditioning use soar power lines topple due wildfires. " can leverage stored energy dispatch when need," Patti Poppe, chief executive PG&E, California' largest utility, said last month.
"It' extraordinary growth," said John Moura, director reliability assessment performance analysis North American Electric Reliability Corporation.
"It' still technology getting used working because system wasn't designed, reliability perspective presents golden opportunity. changes whole paradigm producing electricity, delivering consuming. Storage gives us bit time machine deliver when need."
While scientists clear US, rest world, must radically slash planet-heating emissions electricity generation other sources, rapid growth clean energy such solar wind provides peaks troughs production need actively managed retain reliable grid.
"Batteries can smooth out some variability those times when wind isn't blowing or sun isn't shining. Germans word sort drought: Dunkelflaute," said Moura. "So if you four-hour storage battery, can get you through Dunkelflaute."
Energy storage containers lithium ion batteries University California, San Diego, 16 September 2022 La Jolla, California. Photograph: Sandy Huffaker/AFP/Getty Images
course, wind sun droughts can last longer longest-duration batteries currently available, meaning they panacea. fully clean grid require vast upgrade US transmission lines - example, shift renewable energy swiftly across country where needed. permitting reform allow bitterly contested issue, many environmental groups opposed looser regulations they say only empower fossil fuel concerns.
batteries should able play increasingly strong supporting role energy transition, International Energy Agency last week calling them " key source dispatchable capacity globally". IEA forecasts batteries provide 40% all short-term electricity flexibility needs worldwide 2050.
"There lot changes happening monstrous action still needed if going make energy transition," said Moura.


Bill Gates claimed artificial intelligence help hindrance achieving climate goals, despite growing concern increase new datacentres drain green energy supplies.
philanthropist Microsoft co-founder told journalists AI would enable countries use less energy, even they require datacentres, making technology electricity grids efficient.
Gates downplayed fears AI' climate impact amid mounting concerns tech breakthrough lead rise energy demand require fossil fuels result.
"Let' go overboard," Gates said. "Datacentres, most extreme case, 6% addition [ energy demand] probably only 2% 2.5%. question, AI accelerate 6% reduction? answer : certainly."
query run through AI chatbot tool ChatGPT needs nearly 10 times much electricity process Google search, according estimates Goldman Sachs, mean carbon emissions datacentres double decade between 2022 2030.
Some expert estimates claimed increase number AI datacentres cause electricity demand rise 10% developed countries, after years declining energy due greater efficiency.

" tech companies [ones] willing pay premium, help bootstrap green energy capacity," added.
Breakthrough Energy invested 100 companies involved energy transition. his belief AI ultimately cut carbon unusual. February, peer-reviewed paper Nature Scientific Reports argued generative AI produced between 130 2,900 times less CO2 do simple writing illustration tasks if human had carried them out instead.
AI technology directly affected emissions, well. 2016, just few years after bought British AI lab DeepMind, Google announced had able use lab' nascent deep learning technology cut cooling bill 40% across data centres. stroke, Google said, data centres needed use 15% less electricity spread across all non- tasks result.
power use data centre only part concern carbon impact AI. Microsoft' own emissions reporting, company says "scope three", or indirect, emissions trending wrong direction, part because impact building new datacentres around world - task cannot yet done using renewable electricity.
rise "-device" AI, demonstrated Microsoft through new Copilot+ PCs Apple "Apple Intelligence" boost Siri, muddies water: big companies may able commit buying all electricity renewable sources, they cannot make same promise customers, whose new devices significantly power-hungry they would otherwise.

said delay switch green energy hinder decarbonisation polluting sectors, including heavy industry, making 2050 target reach net zero emissions difficult achieve.
"I worry, general, amount green electricity need transition going show nearly fast need," Gates said.
"If you try map out say: 'Let' get zero 2050,' you're like: 'Another 10 or 15 years might realistic.' It' very hard see. going get zero 2050, I don't think," added.
Gates' warning came week after global report found, despite record rise renewable energy 2023, consumption fossil fuels climbed new record high result steadily rising demand.

weakened target, set out latest energy transition strategy, enable Shell slow pace emissions reductions decade climatologists warned crucial averting climate catastrophe.
Agathe Masson campaign group Reclaim Finance said "retrograde step" showed once again Shell had "no interest acting climate".
Climate experts called all fossil fuel companies reduce emissions energy they sell, known "scope 3 emissions", cutting production oil gas. Shell' targets refer carbon intensity products, rather absolute emissions. means produce gas lower emissions intensity still raise total emissions overall ramps production.
strategy update includes new target reduce scope 3 emissions oil business between 15-20% 2030, compared 2021 levels. set scope 3 emissions target gas business, expected grow 50% 2040.
weaker climate targets set out alongside Shell' annual report, shows chief executive, Wael Sawan, take home pay packet worth almost $10m (£7.94m). His predecessor Ben van Beurden paid £9.7m 2022.
Jonathan Noronha-Gant, senior fossil fuels campaigner NGO Global Witness, said: "Shell' CEO £8m pay packet bitter pill swallow millions workers living high costs energy. Our reliance Shell' dirty oil gas make them rich whilst rest us get poorer."
Last month, Shell revealed annual profit $28bn 2023, one most profitable years record, green activists staged protest outside company' London headquarters.

Shell hpromised 2021 would reduce oil output every rest decade, 2019 peak 1.9m barrels day. claimed had fact achieved target 2021 after $9.5bn sale stake large project Permian basin US. sale helped reduce oil production 1.5m barrels day. Shell said equivalent 200,000 barrels oil gas production last.
company plans start enough fossil fuel projects add 500,000 barrels day oil gas production 2025.
decision continue investing fossil fuels goes against advice climate experts who said there can no new oil or gas developments if world hopes avoid climate crisis.
new oil gas projects would enable Shell "continue providing energy security world needs while delivering cashflow longevity into future", according Sawan.

Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), passed exactly two years ago, pitched policy puts "middle class first". spending bill' residential tax credits so far disproportionately benefited wealthy families, new data indicates.
That' major challenge efforts decarbonize US economy time avert worst consequences climate crisis.
"If going green just niche lifestyle choice upper middle class, won't move needle emissions societal level," said Matt Huber, geography environment professor Syracuse University author 2022 book Climate Change Class War.
treasury department report published month shines light use two IRA renewable energy tax credits: one helped Americans boost energy efficiency homes installing heat pumps, electric water heaters, efficient windows doors, or other upgrades; another helped households install small-scale renewable energy production - most commonly rooftop solar panels.
2023, 3.4m households, representing 2.5% all tax filers, took advantage least one these two subsidies, both expansions pre-existing incentive programs. represents 30% rise use efficiency clean energy tax credits 2021 levels.
Nearly half those who claimed least one these credits last had incomes lower $100,000. Yet roughly 75% tax filers had incomes lower $100,000 2023, closer look use credits households within bracket shows wealthier Americans frequently adopted both tax credits.
all filers making less $100,000, just 0.7% claimed clean energy tax credit, just 0.9% claimed efficiency incentive. -$100,000 bracket, those percentages rose 1.6% stunningly high 4.0%.
dynamic, said Huber, predictable. Tax credit programs can difficult navigate, especially families who can't afford hire tax accountants, said.
Further, though tax credits can make upgrades affordable, they may bring them into reach Americans lower incomes, especially because programs come spending caps each household.
"Most working-class Americans, living paycheck paycheck, do savings or credit buy new heating/cooling system... even complicated incentive do so," said.
tax incentives favor those higher tax burdens. If upgrade eligible $2,000 credits, instance, filers must owe amount or taxes receive full incentive amount.
marked substantial change earlier proposals, would made incentives available even those no tax burden. Lew Daly, senior fellow climate justice group Just Solutions, said " tragic political error" should changed Congress.
"Without refundability, most our country' millions moderate- low-income homeowners intentionally being excluded clean energy transition benefits everyday life, even giving massive fortune tax dollars big corporations affluent households through energy credits program codified," said.
two credits require Americans pay -front cost home upgrades wait until tax season recoup costs - option some households cannot afford.
It' major problem lower-income Americans who grappling rising utility bills "threadbare social safety net", said Daly.
" exclusionary design energy credits program just piling create future worsening inequity," said.
Despite these issues, when compared similar tax incentives pre-dated IRA, distribution these credits even, said James Sallee, energy economist University California, Berkeley. One study showed 60% benefits went top 20% households 2006 2020.
", benefits still regressive," Sallee said. " every income category, money you make, money average people claiming per tax return."
IRA does include provisions aimed promoting equal distribution. renewable energy tax credit, instance, can used enroll community solar - helpful arrangement renters apartment dwellers who tend lower incomes house-owners.
bill includes point--sale rebates efficient appliances upgrades, though rollout slow because they being distributed locally. Only two states yet offer rebates, though others launch programs within months.
Other changes help change distribution tax credits, said Sallee. One them: placing income caps eligibility.
ultimately, said Huber, create green benefits easier all Americans access, they should universal rather means-tested.
"Instead putting out incentives individual households, why work utilities program would aim install heat pumps every household free," asked.
" might sound outlandish, if see solving climate change [] critical public good, there' no reason why decarbonization shouldn't seen core public service like healthcare or education."

UK' net zero economy grew 9% 2023, report revealed, stark contrast 0.1% growth seen economy overall. Nevertheless, report pointed out strong future growth green businesses being put risk government policy reversals, lack investment competition EU US.
Thousands new green companies founded 2023 overall sector responsible production £74bn goods services 765,000 jobs, according report Energy Climate Intelligence Unit (ECIU) Confederation British Industry (CBI).
Hotspots net zero businesses well-paid jobs they provide occur across country, rather being concentrated London south-east, report found. highlighted strong net zero activity some most deprived areas marginal constituencies focal battlegrounds coming general election.
Achieving net zero emissions 2050 vital limiting damage climate crisis. Doing so would only bring economic boost cut energy costs households businesses ensure energy security ending UK' reliance volatile fossil fuel markets, report authors said.
Peter Chalkley, director ECIU, said: "Against backdrop economic stagnation, net zero economy bucking trend, it' clear policy U-turns past damaged investor confidence time when US EU investing billions compete clean industries. question now : political parties provide leadership, stability investment needed generate further growth - or shy away global race net zero?"
Louise Hellem, chief economist CBI, said: " transition net zero presents unprecedented opportunities UK become resilient productive economy while tackling climate change. [Businesses] really don't want see net zero issues politicised."
Rishi Sunak, Conservative prime minister, weakened net zero policies including rollout electric vehicles heat pumps backed new oil gas drilling, against advice scientists. UK recently fallen down index attractiveness renewable energy investment. Keir Starmer, Labour leader, recently halved pledge invest £28bn green economy.
Adriana Curca, manager CBI Economics, said: " quite often tend think costs involved net zero transition, there lot economic opportunities, having real potential drive sustainable long-term growth."
new report analysed green economy, including renewable energy, building energy efficiency, electric vehicles, carbon capture technology green finance. found net zero jobs average £10,000 better paid UK average £35,400, 50% productive, generating £114,300 economic activity overall. number net zero businesses rose 4,000 23,745 2023.
report found hotspots net zero activity some most deprived parts country, including Hartlepool, Nottingham, Redcar Cleveland, all 10% most deprived local authorities. found half top 50 net zero hotspots England Wales top 50 most marginal Conservative seats contested general election, including High Peak, Cheadle, Derby North, Lancaster Wyre.
Adriana Curca: "These [net zero] jobs go long way towards improving living standards those areas."
Thomas Farquhar cleantech business Heatio said: " UK net zero economy offers huge opportunities new, innovative businesses grow. Frustratingly, lagging behind rest Europe."
Farquhar said only 1% UK homes had transitioned low carbon dioxide heating, compared 66% Norway; only 5% suitable UK homes had installed solar panels, compared 16% Netherlands.
UK' National Infrastructure Commission said Friday "government needs move faster ensure necessary [energy] infrastructure can delivered next decade". International Monetary Fund said December "economic benefits accelerating transition low carbon economy vastly outweigh cost".
London School Economics report published January concluded "too much current investment continues unsustainable economy", such oil gas new homes offices energy-efficient.
said: " UK needs increase annual public investment around 1% GDP - £26bn current prices - make decades underinvestment. There upfront investment costs, targeted temporary borrowing good public net investment fiscally responsible."
Department Energy Security Net Zero spokesperson said: " welcome these findings, testament our world-leading plans transform energy industry towards clean renewable energy. clear strategy boost UK industry reach net zero - 80,000 green jobs being supported or pipeline since 2020, £200bn public private low carbon investment since 2010."

rate transition "dirty" green jobs rapidly rising older blue-collar workers being left behind, new research found.
Overall transition rate dirty clean industries increased tenfold between 2005 2021 significant uptick EV-related roles - even before $369bn Inflation Reduction Act boost renewables electric cars.
Yet fewer 1% all workers who leave dirty job appear get another clean or green industry, raising concerns who benefit green transition, according study published National Bureau Economic Research.
Researchers University Pennsylvania analysed data 130m online work profiles - representing approximately 300m job--job transitions - examine how workers may affected shift fossil fuels towards less dirty or carbon-intensive energy sources.
Dirty industries include coal, oil gas extraction or production, well energy-intensive manufacturing jobs like cement, mining, chemical paper pulp.
Older workers ( 40s, 50s 60s) those without college education appear least likely move into green jobs, classified roles clearly associated renewable energy like solar, wind or production electric vehicles.
So far, majority green jobs gone white-collar workers like sales managers, software developers marketing managers. Just quarter (27%) appear gone first-time job seekers, 20,000 jobs went overseas workers.
Overall, one five workers moved one dirty job another. some cities, share dirty--dirty transitions high 90%, suggesting there limited attractive options outside carbon-intensive industries.
some states such Louisiana, Delaware Texas half all transitions out heavy industry or dirty jobs into other dirty jobs, while workers California, Oregon Arizona workers most likely move into EV or clean energy industries. may down heavier concentration fossil-fuel intensive jobs barriers accessing educational opportunities including reskilling.
"Our data suggest, if past trends continue, clean energy transition may important distributional consequences exacerbate underlying trends labor market inequality," authors said.
IRA, Biden' landmark climate legislation passed ago, targets both supply demand EVs renewable technologies, central administration' climate economic policies.
No Republican voted IRA most investment triggered bill funneled into projects GOP-held congressional districts, battery electric vehicle plants popping states such Georgia, Tennessee Texas.
IRA criticised failing require worker protections amid fears landmark funding boost entrench existing inequalities - unless steps taken ensure transition green economy geographically, racially socially just.
"It' tough forecast IRA' effects... likely significant positive effect growth green jobs overall," said lead author Jisung Park, environmental labor economist Penn' School Social Policy & Practice.
" details how IRA implemented targeted likely continue flux, there certainly appears significant interest among policymakers ensuring apply lessons previous employment shocks - including so-called 'China shock' or earlier environmental policies like Clean Air Act - had highly unequal impacts workers across geography, educational background, income," Park added.
Monday, part IRA anniversary tour Biden administration, treasury secretary, Janet Yellen, visits union facility Las Vegas, Nevada - key battleground state 2024 - meet workers learning skills work clean energy projects. She said: "Accelerating these transitions can mean greater demand US clean energy technologies produced American workers. can bolster global clean energy supply chains."

'We've all accepted North Sea declining," says Roy, 70, taxi driver working Aberdeen past 20 years. " years there few huge market crashes always recover. time, it' real decline."
Drive along granite city' Union Street there dozens shuttered shops, some empty almost decade following one longest routs history oil market, brought oil prices lows less $30 (£23) barrel 2016, less half value today.
capital UK' oil gas industry weathered volatility global oil markets since North Sea heyday 1970s; fortunes tied rise fall price Brent crude. now less two weeks away reckoning chancellor, Rachel Reeves, who many expect set out tax changes budget hasten end North Sea industry threaten 200,000 jobs supports.
"They're killing us," says Darren, 50, oil rig worker past 22 years. declines give his full name fear losing his job major oil company. " government going do North Sea what Margaret Thatcher did mines. all know : end UK oil industry. just don't know when."
Labour party stormed power July bold plans end Britain' fossil fuel legacy emerge "clean energy superpower". Within days forming government, party confirmed UK would bring end new licences oil gas projects.
decision cemented Britain' climate leadership crucial decade tackling rising carbon emissions - cold comfort workers such Darren, father two, who fear future industry.
"Imagine losing your job, losing roof your kids' heads, all save carbon emissions drop ocean compared China'? best I can hope hang perhaps four years government voted out," says.
' multibillion-pound paradox'
Alongside plans ban new oil gas licences, Labour came power election pledge toughen windfall tax regime put place after surge market prices following Russia' invasion Ukraine. promised raise energy profits levy increasing headline tax rate companies pay oil gas profits three percentage points, 78%.
Labour plans use higher taxes taken North Sea help fund ambition turn UK into green energy "superpower" line goal making UK' electricity system net zero 2030.
party promised close "loophole" left previous government enabled oil gas firms reduce taxes through investment allowances. These allowances, commonplace tax legislation, allow companies deduct value investments profits - enabling companies pay less tax offering incentive further investment.
combined impact higher headline taxes lower tax relief risks devastating sector' predicted investment second half decade, according industry' trade association, Offshore Energies UK (OEUK). warned changes wipe out £12bn tax receipts jeopardise 35,000 industry jobs.
Stifel, investment bank, believes while move would increase Treasury revenues short term, wider economic impact would mean sharp decline takings 2029 loss 100,000 jobs total.
"There multibillion-pound paradox here," bank said research note. "Any further increases windfall tax take, especially through removal investment allowances, would result substantially lower investment, therefore, lower tax income UK, fewer jobs, loss skills green transition, higher emissions, export jobs, skills UK' energy security other energy-producing countries."
Aberdeen Chamber Commerce warned spectre Labour' budget being felt far beyond energy sector. most recent quarterly economic survey 5,000 companies showed concerns taxation had rocketed risk register; 60% companies north-east Scotland cited government' tax policies barrier growth, 48% just three months ago.
" Treasury understands potential impact," says Russell Borthwick, chamber' chief executive. " I do wonder if they've painted themselves into difficult corner these policies."
" fact, there no windfall profits being made North Sea today. There short period time when Russia invaded Ukraine there oil prices above $100 barrel. Celtic Football Club made bigger profits our largest North Sea oil producers last. looks like they're now being singled out political reasons, rather fiscal reasons," says.
wider regional economy still "inextricably linked oil gas economy", means knock- effect " felt professional services retail industry cab drivers. marked here anywhere else."
Gabby Robertson, 24, worked high-street fashion stores four years noticed decline Aberdeen' retail sector, once flush oil gas money.
" used people come spend £400 £500, see lot less now. People coming buy single items or they tell us they'll come back payday ask paying Klarna ( buy now, pay later platform). It' still better here Union Street. Shops there closed there' nothing replace them. It' getting harder harder small businesses survive," she says.
Economic catastrophe inevitable. Most gloomier projections cited industry assume all investment allowances scrapped, although explicitly proposed government. industry hoping some allowances remain intact help prolong investment declining industry allow workers chance transition Britain' low-carbon future.
'Where transition?'
busy heliport near Aberdeen airport, dozens rig workers wait board helicopters take them 100 miles off Scottish coast giant oil gas platforms extract UK' fossil fuels.
Most work 12- 13-hour days offshore three weeks, before returning homes Scotland north England three-week break. grey October day, bad weather kept many helicopters ground, talk government' upcoming budget darkened mood.
One oil rig worker, aged 53, who asked named, travelled Aberdeen his home Lincolnshire each month past 30 years work North Sea. After only five-day break, returned one-week stint part his plan " fit work while it' around".
"If Rachel Reeves does increase taxes North Sea then that' another hit pockets oil companies operate here. Harbour Energy, one biggest players, let 350 people go part restructuring last. I had hoped keep working until I'm 60 if I can get another five years I should OK," says.
"It' definitely beginning end," says Bob, 52. "Platforms kept producing years longer shut. one I work close two years. It' 12 years I've working rigs after refinery I worked left close. I'll OK because I've had long career I think guys young families who lose jobs just doesn't seem right.
" seems crazy me North Sea faces windfall taxes likes Amazon can get away paying very little. Shouldn't same all companies across country? Isn't fair?"
Paul, rig worker Dundee, one many who fear Britain' plans transition green economy leave behind those who helped build fossil fuel economy past. "What I would ask government : what' proposed solution problem all going create? They talk green transition where' transition us? All I hear 'woke' jargon."

Nuclear power generation likely break records 2025 countries invest reactors fuel shift low-carbon global economy, while renewable energy likely overtake coal power source early next, data shown.
China, India, Korea Europe likely new reactors come stream, while several Japan forecast return generation, French output should increase, according report state global electricity markets published International Energy Agency (IEA) Wednesday.
Electricity demand expected increase around world, fuelled largely move low-carbon economy. Electric vehicles heat pumps, well many low-carbon industrial processes, require electricity rather oil gas.
Continuing rapid growth renewable energy supply means these additional demands likely covered full generation wind, solar other clean energy sources. Renewables make roughly third total electricity generation globally early next.
Dave Jones, insights director energy thinktank Ember, said findings marked potential turning point. "It' beginning end fossil fuel era," said. "Finally, world seems like past peak fossil power, crucial milestone energy transition. [ report] shows 2023 mark end fossil power growth. likely new era declining fossil power."
added: "Peaking enough - need deep rapid CO2 cuts keep within our vanishingly small carbon budget. doing right things, just need do them even faster."
Fatih Birol, IEA' executive director, hailed developments positive sign fight against climate breakdown, though said far effort needed.
" power sector produces carbon dioxide emissions any other world economy, so it' encouraging see rapid growth renewables steady expansion nuclear power course match all increase global electricity demand next three years," said.
" largely thanks huge momentum behind renewables, ever cheaper solar leading way, support important comeback nuclear power. While progress needed, fast, these very promising trends."
IEA' annual analysis market developments policies, Electricity 2024, published Wednesday, showed global electricity demand increased 2.2% 2023, likely reach 3.4% 2024 2026. Most increase expected come rapidly emerging economies:chiefly China, India south-east Asia.
However, IEA warned growth power capacity still uneven around world. instance, while electricity supply increased overall Africa, per capita basis power use across continent remained stagnant three decades.
brake economic social development, people poverty turn polluting sources energy such biomass paraffin. lack readily available electricity holds back children education imperils health whenever hospitals experience blackouts.
Birol said: " international community needs work African governments enable urgent progress [ electricity access] needed."
Africa some world' greatest potential solar wind generation, many vital minerals needed renewable generation components, would- generators face large obstacles form high cost capital renewable energy projects. Many governments urging institutions such World Bank adjust practices make such development easier.

EU must cut greenhouse gas pollution almost three times quickly past decade meet climate targets, European Commission report said.
attempt stop weather growing extreme, EU promised pump 55% less planet-heating gas into air 2030 did 1990. past three decades cut emissions just 32%, leaving behind "significant gaps" next seven years, commission found latest State Energy Union report.
Current policies cut emissions 2030 just 43%, according new estimates European Environment Agency project. figure rises 48% if they include policies planned yet put place, still leave deficit climate action seven percentage points.


" fully achieve these targets, pace emissions reductions needs step," said Wopke Hoekstra, EU' new climate commissioner.
commission report praised efforts quickly ditch gas Russia wake war Ukraine, rapidly pushed energy prices. EU slashed imports Russian gas 155bn cubic metres 2021 80bn cubic metres 2022 estimated 40-45bn cubic metres 2023.
EU oversaw boom clean technologies such wind turbines solar panels, report found, commission cautioned renewable energy needed grow much faster had past decade. average, share renewables European energy grown 0.67 percentage points each hit 21.8% 2021. Reaching EU target 42.5% end decade " require much faster growth coming years", report found.
Emissions falling steadily three things stand out, said Hoekstra. "One, significant emissions cuts needed buildings transport. Progress these sectors sluggish best. Two, natural carbon sink needs grow. certain places carbon sink become source emissions, worrying. three, need much substantial progress cutting emissions agriculture."
Hoekstra, former employee oil firm Shell consultancy McKinsey, said fossil fuel subsidies "unhelpful" clean energy transition reminded national governments promises get rid them.
Most EU member states responded energy crisis making easier people industries buy burn fossil fuels. Subsidies surged €123bn 2022, half do end date decade, report found.
Cop28 climate summit next month EU said push global phaseout unabated fossil fuels - ie those where planet-heating gases emitted captured - certain subsidies.
Hoekstra said: " Cop28 mandate, all member states - I'll say again, all member states - agreed fossil fuel subsidies do address either energy poverty or just transition must phased out soon possible."
Several European governments dragged feet climate action recent months despite prodding EU. Only handful member states met commission' June deadline submitting draft climate energy plans action plans still missing three five biggest polluters: Germany, France Poland.
According new report European branch campaign group Climate Action Network (CAN), policies drafts submitted enough keep planet heating 1.5C above preindustrial levels. They too weak comply minimum EU climate energy requirements 2030.
Chiara Martinelli, director CAN Europe, called governments EU "match magnitude challenge" action plans. " report starkly highlights glaring contrast between urgent demand accelerated climate action sluggish -ground progress."

Joe Biden marked Monday' Earth Day announcing $7bn investment solar energy projects nationwide, focusing disadvantaged communities, unveiling week-long series what White House say "historic climate actions".
president speaking Prince William Forest Park, Triangle, Virginia, touting his environmental record unveiling measures tackle climate crisis increase access, lower costs, clean energy.
centerpiece announcement $7bn grants through Environmental Protection Agency' "solar all" program, funded last year' $369bn Inflation Reduction Act, Biden said benefit hundreds thousands mostly low-income families who currently spend 30% income energy.
"These awards across country [ ] states, territories, tribal governments, municipalities non-profits develop programs enable low income disadvantaged communities benefit residential solar power. it' big deal," said.
"Solar all program means 900,000 households solar rooftops first time soon, millions families save $400 utility bills."
EPA calculated investment, distributed through grants 60 applicant organizations nationwide, generate $8bn household electricity bill savings life program.
Projects funded solar all program create 200,000 jobs, Biden said, advance his Justice40 initiative, least 40% benefits investments federal climate clean energy, affordable sustainable housing projects, directed communities "marginalized underinvestment overburdened pollution".
Biden announced new website encourage citizens join American Climate Corps, volunteer government organization modeled former president Franklin Delano Roosevelt' much-vaunted Civilian Conservation Corps 1930s.
site, climatecorps.gov, aims initially fill 2,000 positions across 36 states, Washington DC Puerto Rico, hosted organizations working clean energy, conservation climate resilience projects. Ultimately corps employ 20,000 young people, White House says.
Aimed mainly young people, administration said press release scheme' objective " make easy any American find work tackling climate crisis while gaining skills necessary clean energy climate resilience workforce future".
"Today historic day landmark achievement," said Democratic New York representative Alexandria Ocasio Cortez, who spoke shortly before Biden took stage.
"[] serves reminder power organizing, what can accomplish when young people, climate advocates, labor organizers working people come together demand future all deserve."
Biden joined independent Vermont senator Bernie Sanders, one architects inflation act, who said climate crisis "existential threat".
"Climate change real, climate change through drought, floods, forest fires, heatwaves extreme weather disturbances already causing massive devastation our country entire world," Sanders said.
"If anyone tells you climate change hoax, them talk farmers whose crop production dwindling, them talk firefighters who risk lives fighting fires severity size we've ever seen."
Today' announcements follow climate measures advanced Biden officials last week, included restricting oil gas leases 13m acres Alaska, finalizing federal land management rule makes conservation equal priority "harmful" private industry activities such oil drilling government-owned assets.
Biden trying shore his support among younger, climate-savvy voters who disappointed administration' approval last oil gas developments including huge Willow project Alaska. Advocates putting pressure Biden declare climate emergency.
said Republicans posed threat climate reforms had initiated, such rejoining Paris agreement Donald Trump withdrew.
"Despite overwhelming devastation red blue states, there still those who deny climate crisis," said. "My Maga Republican friends don't seem think it' crisis, they want repeal Inflation Reduction Act provides funding vast majority these projects, roll back protections clean air clean water.
"Anyone or out government who willfully denies impacts climate change condemning American people very dangerous future, world, I might add."
Biden' Virginia trip first packed Earth Week itinerary visits nationwide administration officials, including transportation secretary, Pete Buttigieg, energy secretary, Jennifer Granholm, officials insist "build stronger, healthier future all".
According White House, Tuesday' theme clean water all communities; Wednesday focus accelerating US' clean transportation future; Thursday focus steps cut pollution power sector strengthen US electricity grid; Friday see measures promote cleaner air healthier schools.

"Broad community-based solar our brightest hope protecting people our climate scourge fossil fuels. These targeted investments mean low-income families get clean energy affordable, resilient protects our ecosystems. It' great see President Biden jumpstart landmark program."

much new coal power capacity China embarked during whole 2022. represented 90% world' new coal plant construction so far.

handful wealthy polluting countries led US spending billions dollars public money unproven climate solutions technologies risk further delaying transition away fossil fuels, new analysis suggests.
These governments handed out almost $30bn subsidies carbon capture fossil hydrogen past 40 years, hundreds billions potentially grabs through new incentives, according new report Oil Change International (OCI), non-profit tracking cost fossil fuels.
date, European Union (EU) plus just four countries - US, Norway, Canada Netherlands - account 95% public handouts CCS hydrogen.
US spent most taxpayer money, some $12bn direct subsidies, according OCI, fossil fuel giants like Exxon hoping secure billions future years.
industry-preferred solutions play limited role curtailing global heating, according Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change (IPCC), being increasingly pushed wealthy nations annual UN climate summit.
carbon capture storage (CCS) projects consistently fail, overspend or underperform, according previous studies. CCS - blue hydrogen projects - rely fossil fuels can lead myriad environmental harms including rise greenhouse gases air pollution.
" United States other governments little show these massive investments carbon capture - none demonstration projects lived initial hype," said Robert Howarth, professor ecology environmental biology Cornell University. " instructive industry itself invests very little carbon capture. whole enterprise dependent government handouts."
time running out curtail climate catastrophe, critics CCS hydrogen say public money should focused proven, less risky solutions such plugging leaky oil wells, energy efficiency buildings, transport electrification renewables speed green transition.
subsidies "colossal waste money", according Harjeet Singh, global engagement director Fossil Fuel Non-Proliferation Treaty Initiative. " nothing short travesty funds meant combat climate change instead bolstering very industries driving."
US Canada spent $4bn subsidize capture CO2 then used extract hard reach oil reserves, process known enhanced oil recovery (EOR), according OCI report shared exclusively Guardian.
However, proponents argue investment needed developing CCS hydrogen technologies, so they can help achieve global climate goals agreed under Paris accords.
"They all part toolset need reach net zero," Astrid Bergmål, state secretary Norway' ministry petroleum energy, told Guardian.
Norway so far approved $6bn subsidies CCS, energy-intensive process powered fossil gas - country expanding.
new analysis based two OCI databases: one tracking public awards distributed companies 1984 2024 carbon capture fossil-based hydrogen research development, well grants pilot commercial projects. other tracks government policies announced since 2020 US, Canada, Australia, EU countries Europe support grants, loans, tax credits, below market insurance plans other financial incentives.
"Governments pouring billions taxpayer dollars into technologies consistently failed deliver promises... allowing fossil fuel companies continue business," said Lorne Stockman, research director OCI.
Subsidies US, world' biggest oil gas producer where estimated three-quarters CO2 currently captured used EOR, top $100bn, according OCI analysis.
thanks new policies Biden administration, particularly landmark climate infrastructure legislation - 2022 Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) - after intense industry lobbying expanded tax benefits both CCS hydrogen few checks balances.
Yet, experts warn CCS technology challenging unlikely deliver.
" history CCS depressing... no significant innovations improved CCS' prospects," said Charles Harvey, professor environmental engineering Massachusetts Institute Technology who co-founded first private CCS startup 15 years ago.
"Nonetheless, again wasting money CCS used instead effectively cut emissions, distracting ourselves necessity moving away fossil fuels, perpetuating polluting industry whose local harms often fall minority economically disadvantaged communities."
Hydrogen, currently mostly used refining oil, fertilizers processing metals foods, green if companies chose use water - gas or coal - raw material, power process renewables fossil fuel.
Yet globally, governments spent $4.2bn projects aim produce blue hydrogen fossil fuels using CCS.
industry claims technology capture 90% 95% CO2, reality, it' closer 12% when every stage energy-intensive process evaluated, according peer-reviewed research scientists Cornell University. " greenhouse gas footprint hydrogen actually greater if simply burn natural gas energy," said Howarth, co-author groundbreaking study.
Canada second largest funder blue hydrogen after US $1.2bn spent date, mostly oil refinery Alberta where hydrogen used upgrading dirty tar sands crude. net CO2 capture rate plant less 70%.
Canadian US government' did respond requests comment.
Globally, governments hand between $500bn $1tn direct fossil fuel subsidies annually, though 2022 true figure closer $7tn - when climate, environmental health costs taken into account, according IMF. $1tn now spent supporting clean energy, according International Energy Agency (IEA) trackers, so amount allocated CCS hydrogen relatively small.
Still, after hottest ever recorded - island nations other developing countries face existential threat sea level rise, desertification, drought, extreme heat, wildfires floods - only $700m pledged governments new loss damage fund Cop28 - far short estimated $400bn needed annually. financial shortfall climate adaptation runs into hundreds billions - rising.
Singh, director Fossil Fuel Non-Proliferation Treaty Initiative, said: "While investing billions technologies further entrench fossil fuel use, developed countries simultaneously neglect moral financial responsibilities fund crucial efforts vulnerable communities... that' grim irony."
While decades-long reputation CCS largely one "underperformance" "unmet expectations", according IEA 2023, many experts agree play role reducing emissions polluting industries such cement, steel chemicals.
according Chris Bataille, IPCC expert decarbonizing heavy industry, subsidies must come conditions target products, processes, order achieve just economically sound green transition.
"I don't think should directly subsidizing CCS ever... should directly subsidize clean things useful people. Staggered subsidies clean iron key making steel, clean ammonia key fertilizers clean clinker cement, would channel market - whole idea government regulation.
"Companies designed make profits, they only consider what priced, so subsidies should come conditions, including mandatory net-zero transformation plans," added Batallie, who adjunct research fellow Columbia University center global energy policy.

government approved $2.6bn subsidies requested 2020, vast majority allocated Porthos project - incentivize some oil majors chemical companies Port Rotterdam store captured CO2 empty North Sea offshore gas field. ( figure doesn't include subsidies approved 2022 or requested 2023.)
spokesperson Dutch climate ministry said final amount paid out expected substantially lower depends market price carbon project costs, these fossil fuel-powered technologies key country' green transition.
" Netherlands, safeguards built subsidies CCS, so these do come cost alternative, clean energy technologies. Dutch policy aims minimizing future role fossil fuels energy system set conditions time horizon fossil CCS support."
Climate advocates say phase-out too slow.
" money should used get away fossil fuels making industrial processes green, rather these false solutions cash cow industry - part because governments take risks market fluctuations price carbon," said Maarten de Zeeuw, climate energy campaigner Greenpeace Netherlands.
carbon dioxide capture unit attached biomass generator greenhouse Egchel, Netherlands. Photograph: Piroschka van de Wouw/Reuters
Norway' first full-scale heavily subsidized CCS project attached Mongstad oil refinery described 2007 then prime minister, Jens Stoltenberg, country' "moon landing". project failed, part due rising costs, though did spawn CCS test facility government said "instrumental technology development".
country' current flagship CCS project, Longship, involves capturing CO2 waste incineration cement production shipped stored offshore. Costs here rising - though, according state secretary Bergmål, rising inflation supply chain instabilities affecting other climate technologies.
OCI claims Norway expanding CCS justify oil gas expansion use producing blue hydrogen export Europe - where enjoying renewed interest despite evidence viability alternative fuel limited.
