China off track all core 2025 climate targets, despite fact clean energy now biggest driver country' economic growth, analysis found.
After years extraordinarily rapid growth, China now grappling slowdown causing ripples internally internationally. government supercharged growth renewable energy industry simultaneously poured stimulus funds into construction manufacturing, continues approve coal power.
China' total energy consumption increased 5.7% 2023, first moment since 2005 demand energy grew faster GDP. China' economy grew 5.2% last, rate would rapid most countries slow comparison previous rates growth.
carbon dioxide emissions continued grow, even economic growth slowed due fact China' economic growth during after Covid-19 pandemic highly energy intensive. Between 2021 2023, CO2 emissions grew average 3.8%, 0.9% between 2016 2020. GDP growth slowed slightly same period.

findings published analysis Carbon Brief conducted Lauri Myllyvirta, lead analyst Centre Research Energy Clean Air (CREA).
Under Paris agreement, China' climate pledges require number targets met 2025; these include increasing share non-fossil energy sources 20% reducing carbon intensity economy 18%. Carbon intensity refers how many grams CO2 released produce kilowatt hour electricity.
However, Myllyvirta' analysis found China "way off track" many these targets, primarily because carbon intensity recent economic growth. CO2 emissions fall between 4% 6% meet government' 2025 target, Myllyvirta predicts.
Part problem stems fact during Covid-19 pandemic, government supported economy injecting stimulus measures into construction manufacturing sector, "shifting pattern growth into carbon-intensive direction", said Myllyvirta. same time, many other countries targeted stimulus measures towards households, driving demand consumer goods, resulting export boom China.
impossible do, said Myllyvirta: "Optimistically, these distortions economic structure before during zero-Covid reversed, combined clean energy drive serve bring down emissions rapidly."
Around one percentage point China' CO2 emissions growth last came manufacturing clean energy technologies, such solar PV, electric vehicles batteries. These products ultimately lead net drop emissions, although some felt outside China goods exported.
Part challenge understanding China' energy transition numbers involved every sector global significance. Despite fact, 2023, China commissioned much solar PV entire world did 2022, according International Energy Agency, rapid growth energy demand recent years outstripped clean energy additions.
current trend increased energy demands, energy production non-fossil sources would need grow 11% meet 2025 target. Currently, renewable energy production increasing annual rate 8.5%. Clean energy contributed $1.6tn (£1.26tn) China' economy 2023, accounting all growth investment.
target reducing carbon intensity China' economy 18% 2025, China so far managed only 5% reduction since 2020. means CO2 emissions come down absolute terms 2023 2025 meet target.
According National Energy Administration, China' installed capacity renewable energy exceeded 1.45bn kilowatts 2023, accounting half country' total installed power generation capacity.
separate report published Thursday CREA Global Energy Monitor found China approved 114 gigawatts (GW) coal power 2023, 104 GW 2022. China' share global coal emissions surpassed 64% 2023.
Since Xi Jinping, China' leader, pledged "strictly control" new coal power 2021, approval new coal actually increased rapidly. Nearly half growth power generation between 2020 2023 came coal. partly because some thermal power developers government officials see decade window opportunity CO2 emissions can continue increase before 2030, when Xi promised they peak. Still, China widely expected meet target 2025.
Local governments, particularly mining provinces, often rely investment coalmines thermal power sources boost GDP figures.
Myllyvirta said: " major acceleration coal consumption growth approvals new coal power plants taken place since President Xi made these pledges 2021 contradicts commitments - China needs take determined action 2024-25 avoid failing respect them."

world off track meet climate goals public blame, Darren Woods, chief executive oil giant ExxonMobil, claimed - prompting backlash climate experts.
world' largest investor-owned oil company, Exxon among top contributors global planet-heating greenhouse gas emissions. interview, published Tuesday, Woods argued big oil primarily responsible climate crisis.
real issue, Woods said, clean-energy transition may prove too expensive consumers' liking.
" dirty secret nobody talks how much all going cost who' willing pay," told Fortune last week. " people who generating those emissions need aware pay price generating those emissions. ultimately how you solve problem."
Woods said world " path" cut planet-heating emissions net zero 2050, scientists say imperative avoid catastrophic impacts global heating. "When people going willing pay carbon reduction?" said Woods, who Exxon' chief executive since 2017.
" opportunities make fuels lower carbon, people aren't willing spend money do."
Experts say Woods' rhetoric part larger attempt skirt climate accountability. No new major oil gas infrastructure can built if world avoid breaching agreed temperature limits Exxon, along other major oil companies currently basking record profits, pushing ahead aggressive fossil-fuel expansion plans.
"It' like drug lord blaming everyone himself drug problems," said Gernot Wagner, climate economist Columbia business school.
"I hate tell you, you're chief executive largest publicly traded oil company, you influence, you make decisions matter. Exxon mercy markets they shaping them, they shaping policy. So no, you can't blame public failure fix climate change."
Troves internal documents analyses past decade established Exxon knew dangers global heating far back 1970s, forcefully successfully worked sow doubt climate crisis stymie action clamp down fossil fuel usage. revelations inspired litigation against Exxon across US.
"What they're really trying do whitewash own history, make invisible," said Robert Brulle, environment policy expert Brown University who researched climate disinformation spread fossil-fuel industry.
2021 analysis demonstrated Exxon had downplayed own role climate crisis decades public-facing messaging.
" playbook : sell consumers product you know dangerous, while publicly denying or downplaying those dangers. Then, when dangers no longer deniable, deny responsibility blame consumer," said Naomi Oreskes, Harvard historian science co-author 2021 paper.
Last, another study co-authored Oreskes found Exxon' own scientists "correctly skillfully" predicted trajectory global warming, then spent decades sowing doubt climate science policies order protect business model.
Tuesday interview, however, Woods says world "waited too long" develop carbon-free technologies. said Exxon "recognized need decarbonize" carbon tax would help achieve, while defending oil giant' comparatively meager investment renewable energy, pointing focus upon nascent technologies, such carbon capture hydrogen fuels.
Exxon does "see ability generate above-average returns investors" established clean energy generation such wind solar, Woods said.
" recognize need. just don't see appropriate use ExxonMobil' capabilities," added.
Woods does mention his company lobbied fend off provisions earlier version legislation would levied heavy taxes polluting companies pay climate efforts, or top Exxon lobbyist filmed saying firm' support carbon tax public relations strategy meant stall serious climate policies.
" decades, they told us science too uncertain justify action, premature act, should wait see how things developed," said Oreskes. "Now CEO says: oh dear, we've waited too long. If isn't gaslighting, I don't know what."
Wagner said Exxon touting ambition slash emissions own operations while betting rest world won't do same, order continue selling oil.
" can't both ways saying ' energy company' then basically ignoring cheapest source electricity history something Exxon should investing," said.
video interview comes Exxon pursuing lawsuit against activist shareholders who aiming push Exxon take stricter environmental standards. Those shareholders, Woods said, trying stop Exxon' central business model selling oil gas, won't accede.
" want cater shareholders who real investors, who interest seeing company succeed generating return investments," said. " don't feel responsibility activists hijack process... frankly, abuse advance ideology."
Exxon received subsidies build out clean energy business 2022 Inflation Reduction Act, Fortune chief executive Alan Murray pointed out interview. Woods argued "building business government subsidy long-term sustainable strategy".
" way government incentivized trying catalyze investments space through subsidies," said. "Driving significant investments scale even gets close moving needle going cost lot money."
vast majority Exxon' own investments still being put toward fossil fuel expansion, said Brulle.
" what they do: they're going basically blame victim, American public," said.
"They spend fossil fuels they spend billions trying influence public opinion, supposed foot bill damage."

problem: rising carbon dioxide atmosphere
level carbon dioxide, main greenhouse gas, rising since Industrial Revolution highest 4m years. rate rise even striking, fastest 66m years, scientists saying world "uncharted territory".
causes (I): burning fossil fuels
Billions tonnes CO2 still pumped into atmosphere every coal, oil gas burning. slight reduction 2020 result coronavirus lockdowns no "tiny blip", emissions since resumed upward trajectory.
causes (II): forest destruction
felling forests timber, beef, soy palm oil big contributor CO2 emissions. significant cause annihilation wildlife.
causes (III): methane emissions
Methane potent greenhouse gas causes 25% all global heating today. Fossil fuel extraction, cattle, rice paddies landfills responsible. There "scary" surge methane emissions since 2007, according scientists, potentially due wetlands heating.
consequences: global temperature rise
planet' average temperature started climb steadily two centuries ago rocketed since second world war consumption population risen. Many records smashed 2023, hottest record. Global heating means there energy atmosphere, making extreme weather events frequent intense.
consequences: rising sea levels
Sea levels inexorably rising ice land melts hotter oceans expand. Sea levels slow respond global heating, so even if temperature rise restricted 2C above pre-industrial levels, one five people world eventually experience cities being submerged, New York London Shanghai.
consequences: shrinking Arctic sea ice
heating melts sea ice, reveals darker water absorbs sun' heat, causing heating - one example vicious circles climate system. Scientists think changes Arctic may responsible worsened heatwaves floods Eurasia North America.
upside (I): wind solar energy soaring
Huge drops cost helped renewable energy become cheapest electricity most places exponential growth expected continue. action support still required reach scale needed, especially developing countries.
upside (II): electric vehicles
global fleet electric cars vans still small compared those vehicles running fossil fuels. sales growing extremely quickly tipping points EV adoption already reached several countries. Electric cars cheaper own run, suggesting they replace combustion engine vehicles.
upside (III): battery costs
Plummeting battery costs only increase electric car sales can enable renewable energy stored while sun shining wind blows. other technologies longer storage duration, such green hydrogen, needed.

UK households face higher energy bills next, after leading forecaster said electricity market prices rise 2024 - remain well above pre-energy crisis levels rest decade.
Analysts Cornwall Insight predicted Britain' wholesale power prices would rise average £96.64 per megawatt-hour so far £129/MWh next, due higher gas prices triggered war Ukraine.
analysis undertaken based commodity futures markets end September. Since then, gas electricity markets climbed further due fears Israel-Hamas war pose risk oil gas supplies.
Electricity prices forecast stay elevated until end decade, remaining least 60% higher they 2021 before global economic recovery Covid-19 pandemic caused markets climb.
analysts expect renewable energy connects grid, reducing reliance gas-fired power plants, market prices begin fall. However, they stay above £80/MWh - well above pre-crisis levels.
Tom Edwards, senior modeller Cornwall Insight, said slowing UK' shift towards renewable energy keep energy bills higher longer. Cornwall' latest report found UK likely miss target increase offshore wind fivefold 50GW 2030 due rising costs supply chain.
"Rolling back our net zero ambitions slowing our transition away fossil fuels likely costly delay only see us fall further behind decarbonising country leave consumers shouldering prolonged burden high prices," Edwards said.
"Without resolute commitment greener sustainable future, achieving net zero emissions pre-crisis energy bills becomes increasingly elusive goal," added.
Cornwall forecasts 2030 offshore wind make 26% UK' installed electricity capacity, 13% today. Meanwhile, share gas power plants fall 27% UK' total power capacity 13% 2030.
" can see country travelling right direction towards renewables-based electricity system. However, our estimates continue show simply fast enough deliver government targets," Edwards said.
cost gas electricity wholesale energy markets makes just 50% typical household bill. rest bill includes charges pay grid upgrades, needed connect new renewables, cost government policies such supporting low-carbon energy projects.
"Time essence, critical government reassess commitment accelerating renewable energy adoption, includes being flexible when setting auction parameters reach UK' offshore wind goals," Edwards said.

EU aims slash net greenhouse gas pollution 90% 2040 part push become world' first climate-neutral continent, European Commission announced before elections June.
Under landmark proposal laid out executive body Tuesday, bloc pump 90% less planet-heating gas into air 2040 did 1990, figure includes carbon removes atmosphere.
target bottom end 90-95% net cuts EU' scientific watchdog recommended June, meeting would require much faster shift clean economy. three decades between 1990 2021, EU' 27 member states cut emissions just 30%, according European Environment Agency, leaving them half long do twice work.
Wopke Hoekstra, EU climate commissioner, said target sent message world Europe "continues lead way" climate action. "Tackling climate crisis marathon, sprint. need make sure everyone crosses finish line nobody left behind," said.
target, recommendation next commission after European elections June, part bid stop extreme weather growing violent. some individual targets scrapped last minute because protests farmers seen politicians EU member states row back plans make farms pollute less.
Pieter de Pous, nature policy expert climate thinktank E3G, said: "It' hard find fitting use metaphor 'cutting off branch you sit on' witnessing farmers unite efforts undermine very climate policies designed protect them devastating impacts climate change."
final document no longer refers cutting agricultural emissions methane, powerful greenhouse gas belched out cows, or nitrogen, pollutant fertiliser manure reacts form heat-trapping gases. text instead states purpose launch political debate. " does propose new policy measures or set new sector-specific targets."
scrapped farming target third concession farmers last week, after Ursula von der Leyen, European Commission president, told lawmakers Tuesday morning she would take back proposal use fewer pesticides. Last week she asked member states delay key rule save wildlife protect soils.
EU sped energy transition recent years building sources clean electricity, such wind turbines solar panels, faster pace. Under new proposal, plans use 80% less fossil fuels energy sector 2040 did 2021.
made little progress cleaning sectors like food transport.
Keeping exemption agriculture sector "sets worrisome precedent negotiations climate ambition begin", said De Pous.
2040 target announced Tuesday European parliament alongside new strategy capture carbon. commission had previously proposed target capture 50m tonnes CO2 2030 wants rise 280m tonnes 2040.
then, commission suggested, EU should emit less 850 megatonnes CO2e remove 400 megatonnes land industry.
Kadri Simon, EU energy commissioner, said renewables energy efficiency still "central" climate neutrality reaching net zero emissions meant using technologies manage carbon.
Environmental groups scientists divided how big role capturing carbon factories removing atmosphere should play energy transition. Experts said technology most promising way clean some heavy industries like cement, compensate leftover emissions sectors hard clean.
promise carbon capture future used governments oil companies keep burning fuels pollute air other toxic particles, allow them cut emissions slowly today.

She added: "Without honesty end fossil fuels finally tackling farming emissions, it' hard see how EU even clear too-low bar it' setting itself."

former deputy RenewableUK replace Chris Stark, who appointed lead government' "mission control centre" clean energy, Covid vaccine-style taskforce aimed delivering clean cheaper power 2030.
Pinchbeck tapped role earlier told Guardian August "change can quite frightening" rural communities likely host renewable energy infrastructure.
Ed Miliband, energy secretary, said Pinchbeck would join CCC " pivotal point" UK' journey net zero, halfway through "decisive decade halt climate change".
"Following her leadership Energy UK, [Pinchbeck] well placed advise challenge government our net zero goals - ensuring meet our climate commitments ambition urgency," Miliband said.
added Pinchbeck' "extensive experience" leading decarbonisation energy industry underscores "how economics clean energy now aligned climate policy, driving both environmental protection economic growth".
Pinchbeck appointed Energy UK' chief executive January 2020 months before Covid-19 pandemic led record low energy market prices wiped billions market value major energy companies. sharp bounceback energy prices 2021 caused 30 energy suppliers go bust. Russia' invasion Ukraine early 2022 pushed energy prices across Europe further forced UK government step subsidise bills.
Pinchbeck said: "Energy moved fringes very centre economic policy I hope I played my part making sure importance our sector energy transition clear government.
"I am excited bring my experience knowledge decade energy challenge decarbonising our economy speed tangible benefits people UK."

UK' transition net zero under threat delays approving new infrastructure put billions pounds investment offshore wind schemes other vital upgrades risk, big ports said.
British Ports Association (BPA) written government Labour calling action clear backlog harbour orders, legislation needed ports make infrastructure changes support offshore wind projects.
shadow energy security secretary, Ed Miliband, week promised Labour take lead global efforts tackle climate crisis if wins Thursday' election.

Port authorities hope take advantage growth offshore wind industry - seen vital hitting UK' legal commitment net zero carbon emissions 2050 - upgrading infrastructure allow assembly maintenance turbines site.
Some ports, however, waiting nearly four years orders signed off, four times longer expected, while other schemes failed get off ground fears delays make them unviable.
BPA, whose members own operate 350 ports facilities, estimated take nearly nine years clear all existing orders, while saying applications likely increase number coming years meet growing demand offshore wind schemes.
Harbour orders legislative consents given ports construct new infrastructure Marine Management Organisation (MMO), public body.
Ports require harbour orders give them new legal powers, enabling them borrow funds or enter joint ventures, essential planning facilities such upgrades allow work turbines or cruise terminals.
BPA director policy external affairs, Mark Simmonds, said significant port development needed coming years meet growing demand offshore wind, well growth cargo cruises.
" extremely regrettable single point failure processing relatively simple consents regulator can threaten billions pounds port investment ultimately put our net zero ambitions risk," said.
BPA told Labour £1.8bn manifesto pledge upgrade UK ports held back unless immediate action taken speed consents.
number approvals dropped off significantly, only four harbour orders receiving green light past two years, compared nine 2021.
Falmouth Harbour, waiting almost two years secure new order, said delays preventing progressing plans tap into floating offshore wind projects Celtic Sea.
harbour' chief executive, Miles Carden, said: " significant time delays massive distraction."
Port Southampton submitted application new powers June 2020 still yet secure order, Port Plymouth waiting three years MMO approval. Industry experts believe body' lack legal resources behind delays.
Associated British Ports, country' biggest port operator, said delays unacceptable holding number important operational improvement projects.
Tim Morris ABP said: "Without capacity MMO or alternative routes process applications, potential ABP other port operators grow our capability handle trade Britain needs play greater role energy transition risks being lost."
Offshore wind provided 17% UK' energy 2023, government targeting 50GW 2030.
report Renewable UK last estimated investment around £4bn would needed ports produce 34GW energy floating offshore wind 2040.
Lara Moore, head marine law firm Ashfords, said aware projects failing early stage because they unlikely obtain consents within two- three- window they needed become operational.
" offshore wind customers who trying bring turbines, you've got able react. You can't say prospective customer, 'yes, really interested can't start another four years because waiting consent'," she said.
spokesperson MMO said application process typically takes three years.
" present volume applications resulting perceived delays created large volume applications submitted ahead two price increases harbour order fees 2022 2023," spokesperson said.
" processing these applications complex time consuming MMO presently fully cost recovering work undertaken function."
Department Transport said working body ports improve process.

When Bryn Hawkins worked coalmines through 1960s early 1970s says few understood impact burning fossil fuels would planet.
Now, public officials hoping disused mines provided millions tonnes fossil fuels used potential source renewable energy across country.
already success Gateshead, where council-owned Gateshead Energy Company using warm water extensive network old mine workings 150 metres below town supply heat hot water.
Other authorities catching potential metro mayor west England, Dan Norris, betting £1.5m some 100-plus mines Somerset South Gloucestershire able provide renewable source heat region.
Speaking launch mapping study former mining town Radstock, Somerset, Hawkins, 82, buoyed prospect mines provided livelihood him his family being repurposed benefit environment.
"It' wonderful," says. " my lifetime, concerns environment come relatively recently. didn't think anything smoke going out chimneys. now there' interest, attention - I understand little bit."
When mines running they pumped dry so miners access coal. When they closed, pumps turned off mines flooded again.
water heated geothermal energy, heat Earth' core, some places can reach temperatures 20C.
Dan Mallin Martin, hydrogeologist Coal Authority, public body responsible managing effects past coalmining, says naturally heated water can brought through shafts or boreholes passed through heat exchanger - extracting heat water.
"Typically can take out around five 8C, doesn't sound like lot, if you pass heat pump, effectively fridge reverse, boosts temperature something can use - something like 60 or 70 degrees centigrade - can go into people' homes, people' businesses, into hospitals, heat networks, many different end users.
"That' great way make use they can really efficient. really low carbon."
process no net water consumption - does take out uses - mine water put back into flooded mines recycled.
" transition heat pumps energy source very important that' one our options decarbonising our heating requirements across UK," Mallin Martin said. " heat pumps, ground source options mine water, can feed into decarbonisation, especially if couple green electricity like solar panels wind."
Gateshead Energy Company powers centralised heat pump solar farm estimates saving 1,800 tonnes CO2.
There international examples tech being used successfully. mine water project Heerlen, Netherlands, described "poster child" innovation.
"There' real green incentive make most certainly supports levelling agenda communities coalfield delivering secure, local, low-carbon heat," said Mallin Martin.
Like lot nascent energy solutions, political key. Norris, who mayor west England since 2021 mining family, committed £1.5m mapping study establish extent mine network Somerset South Gloucestershire. Once established, authority zone most viable locations further tests.
" synergy fantastic mines contributed CO2 emissions now hold key clean, green energy," Norris said. "Heat mines national, possibly global, implications too.
"If cost living crisis tells us anything, tells us cost energy critical, just poorest our communities, actually everybody businesses well. So got make cost energy much cheaper.
" I believe environmentally important way producing energy, cost-effective way producing energy can heat our economy help cool down our planet I'm excited."

Nearly half experts surveyed climate thinktank believe China' carbon dioxide emissions already peaked, or do so 2025, reflecting increasing optimism country' green transition time when being called take leading position global climate action.
According report published Tuesday Centre Research Energy Clean Air (CREA), research organisation, 44% climate experts academia industry believe China' CO2 emissions peak, latest, 2025. last year' survey, only 21% experts gave same response.
There optimism China reducing reliance coal. Asked if they believed coal consumption had already peaked, 36% experts said yes, 20% last.
China' official targets reach peak carbon emissions 2030 achieve carbon neutrality 2060. Beijing pledged "strictly control" use coal during 14th "five- plan", covers period until end 2025.
Coal accounts nearly 80% China' fossil fuel emissions.
"China already plans reduce coal consumption after 2026 cut definitely pretty drastic fast if China wants achieve 2060 carbon neutrality goal," said Wang Xiaojun, founder People Asia Climate Solutions, climate NGO based Manila.
leaders' concerns energy security, intensified after several parts China experienced power blackouts 2021 2022, after war Ukraine destabilised oil supplies, means "China yet talked coal phase-out", said Wang. despite fact energy experts argue greater diversity China' energy mix improvements renewable energy infrastructure effective ways ensuring energy security, without relying backup coal-fired power plants.
China world leader when comes investing clean energy. Previous analysis Lauri Myllyvirta, lead analyst CREA, found clean energy contributed record 11.4tn yuan (£154.4bn) China' economy last, biggest driver GDP growth.
past two years, China focused rebuilding post-Covid economy pivoting towards hi-tech, green industries. These called "new three": solar panels, electric vehicles batteries, attracted huge levels investment.
Shi Xunpeng, one CREA report' authors, said: "Clean energy industries emerged key drivers economic growth. China continues transition, benefits becoming increasingly clear."
Analysts focused whether China can reduce carbon intensity economy - amount CO2 released produce single unit electricity.
" order align Paris agreement... China need either speed renewable energy deployment even further or guide economic development less energy-intensive direction," said Myllyvirta.
country responsible 90% growth CO2 emissions since 2015, China' plans shape future global transition. country generally conservative when comes targets. Anders Hove, senior research fellow, Oxford Institute Energy Studies, said: "It' unlikely there any commitment setting quantitative emissions target goes beyond present policies, let alone quantifying decline total emissions."
China' approach climate targets : "If you say, do. If you can't do, don't say," Hove said. "Whereas many foreign leaders see value aspirational goals, infrequently dial them back when economics or political winds change, viewed negative light Chinese side."
article amended 27 November 2024. earlier version said People Asia Climate Solutions based Shanxi, China, when based Manila.

increasing use renewable energy, US only slashed planet-warming emissions improved air quality, yielding hundreds billions dollars benefits, new report found.
study, published Cell Reports Sustainability Wednesday based publicly available data, focuses uptick renewable energy US 2019 2022.
" 2019 through 2022, wind solar generation increased 55%," said Dev Millstein, scientist Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. " 2022, wind solar provided roughly 14% total electricity needs US."
During time period, reducing use fossil fuel power plants, nation' use wind solar power cut carbon dioxide emissions 900m metric tons, authors found. That' equivalent taking 71m cars off road every.
Those major climate benefits can obscure air quality benefits renewable power yielded, wrote authors, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory renewable consulting firm Clean Kilowatts. illuminate those co-benefits, researchers quantified how much use wind solar reduced toxic air emissions, focusing specifically sulfur dioxide (SO2) nitrogen dioxides (NOx), both produced during fossil fuel combustion.
They found emissions SO2 NOx - both linked increased asthma risk variety other health issues - decreased total 1m metric tons three- period.
determine impact reduction public health, authors "used air quality models track population exposed pollution power plants", Millstein said. They employed epidemiological research examine effects those emissions, quantified benefits using Environmental Protection Agency dollar value establishing value reducing risk early death across population, said.
All told, emission reductions SO2 NOx provided $249bn climate health benefits US, authors found - figure Millstein said found "noteworthy".
study went examine benefits wind solar offer particular regions United States. Wind power, instance, particularly beneficial across Central states due displaced emissions local power grids; same true solar power Carolinas. It' aspect research Jeremiah Johnson, climate energy professor North Carolina State University, who did work study, applauded.
"These findings can help us target future wind solar development provide greatest climate health benefits," said Johnson, whose work cited study.
said hopes paper helps public focus benefits wind solar already creating.
public " often focused challenges face" when comes ecological damage, said. " important recognize when something working."

Major fossil fuel companies committed tens millions pounds funding UK universities since 2022, can revealed, despite many these institutions having actively pledged divest oil gas.
According freedom information requests submitted climate journalism site DeSmog, £40.4m research agreements, tuition fees, scholarships, grants consultancy fees pledged 44 UK universities 32 oil, coal gas companies since 2022.
largest contributors Shell, Malaysian state-owned oil company Petronas, BP. These three companies account 76% total figure awarded, having given £20.98m, £5.19m £4.89m respectively.
further 10 companies made nearly 20% remaining contributions during period: Sinopec, Equinor, BHP Group, Total Energies, Eni SPA, Saudi Aramco, ExxonMobil, Kellas Midstream, Ithaca Energy Chevron.
Previous reporting openDemocracy Guardian found between 2017 December 2021, £89m funding given UK universities some world' biggest fossil fuel companies.
These partnerships shown no sign abating, DeSmog' research shows additional £40m pledged since 2022, even after 102 higher education institutions promised stop taking funding fossil fuel industry.
top 10 beneficiaries fossil fuel financial commitments since 2022
Green party MP Caroline Lucas said: "Young people care so deeply protecting planet because futures line. Yet fossil fuel giants putting future risk planet-wrecking pollution, then attempting youthwash reputation handing dirty money universities.
"If going tackle climate emergency secure livable future next generation, educational institutions should cut all ties fossil fuel companies immediately."
figures do include amount held fossil fuel investments universities. Research DeSmog indicates least 18 higher education institutions held direct investments 25 fossil fuel companies relevant time period, collectively worth further £8.1m.
Many top universities hold stakes high-value pooled investment funds pouring hundreds millions into fossil fuel companies. Research conducted student campaign group People & Planet estimates, July 2022, much £319m still held these funds universities across UK, including some institutions made promises divest.
65% country' higher education institutions refused make further fossil fuel investments, potentially removing £17.7bn reach industry, while 51 universities yet divest oil gas.
Laura Clayson, climate campaigns manager People & Planet, said: " say those 51 universities left divest: student movement remain unwavering demands justice until our victory list includes every single one you."
University Exeter received most fossil fuel firms since 2022, having signed £14.7m, five- deal Shell November project "carbon storage sequestration".
According contract award notice, project part "wider Shell-led research programme focused sequestration aligns Shell' target net zero emissions energy business 2050".
Last, Shell produced only 0.02% energy renewable sources, dropped target cut oil production.
company recently abandoned plans cut oil production 1%-2% each until 2030, investing £33bn oil gas production between 2023 2035, compared just £8bn-£12bn "low-carbon" products.
Shell claims reduced oil production quickly expected, though company' planned emissions between 2018 2030 estimated account nearly 1.6% global carbon budget.
Shell declined comment. University Exeter spokesperson said work Shell would "contribute global race net zero".
Recent research University Queensland shows half world' top fossil fuel producers fail meet climate targets unless they expand plans decarbonise. UN report said world miss climate targets unless commits "phasing out all unabated fossil fuels".
Imperial College London received second-highest amount funding fossil fuel firms since 2022, £6.7m, having already received £54m oil gas companies between 2017 2021.
spokesperson Imperial said pledged 2020 only engage research partnerships " fossil fuel companies where research forms part plans decarbonisation, only if company demonstrates credible strategic commitment achieving net zero 2050".
university maintained working relationship 13 fossil fuel companies since 2022.
University Manchester houses International Centre Advanced Materials research unit, collaboration between BP leading universities UK US, including Manchester, Cambridge Imperial. centre' website says supports "BP' ambitions become net zero company 2050".
BP generated just 0.17% energy renewable sources 2022. first half last, company spent 10 times amount new oil gas projects did "low carbon" energy. 2022, 92.7% all activity BP Shell went into fossil fuel investment.
Shell, BP posted record profits 2022, £23bn, while scaling back plans cut emissions 2050, saying needed keep investing new oil gas meet consumer demand. BP did respond request comment.
University Manchester' funding agreements BP stretch back 2008, when selected company run Projects Engineering College. university received money Shell TotalEnergies.
spokesperson institution said: "Since 2019 all new research funded BP ICAM focused topics materials sciences support energy transition, providing research support BP' goal become net zero company 2050."
spokesperson Heriot-Watt, received third-largest amount funding, just £6m, said university committed rapid just energy transition, led our world-class research teaching. work collaboration energy sector develop education research opportunities related net zero, responsible consumption oil gas, transition renewable energy sources."
Fossil fuel companies pledged fund scholarships tuition fees across least 17 universities since 2022.

Western Australia' greenhouse gas emissions rising little sign main power grid can decarbonise rapidly, leaving other states make bigger cuts if Australia' legislated carbon reduction goals met.
Modelling results presented state government late last obtained Guardian Australia showed state' carbon emissions 2024 track reach 91.5m tonnes carbon dioxide-equivalent, or 20% above 2005 levels.
Australia' emissions reached 459m tonnes CO2-e December 2023, according preliminary federal data. tally 26% below 2005 levels still well shy legislated 43% reduction target.
Unlike others states, WA does own 2030 target. Labor-led Cook government considering legislating goal 2035 once sees what federal government settles.
One scenario put forward modelling Climateworks CSIRO had main power grid serving bulk WA' population reducing 2005-level emissions 2% 2030 20% 2035 based present policies. Those "significant emissions reductions" South West Interconnected System, would need overcome "key barriers", including lack transmission capacity.
Brad Pettitt, sole Greens MP state parliament, said WA lagged other states even though government-owned grid offered means expedite take- other states such Victoria lacked. Any 2035 goal unlikely agreed WA government before 2026 earliest.
" 10% Australia' population 17% emissions," Pettitt said. " huge outlier.
"What' disturbing no serious plan get them down decade. Nothing planned all."
State-owned Western Power, instance, takes five years grant approvals new wind solar farms connected grid. Even if they do, developers stump $100,000 per megawatt capacity pay transmission they then hand state, said.
WA' relatively high dependence growing mining gas sector, including downstream processing raw materials, resulted carbon emissions rising even other states federal government introduce policies cut pollution.
WA Northern Territory connected national electricity market serves most rest country, states exporting importing power between them.
Gas plays bigger role WA power supply elsewhere, making 38% Swis electricity wholesale market last June, ahead coal' share 27%, wind' 16% solar farms less 2%. 38% WA homes rooftop solar, supplying 15.5% rising capacity 1 megawatt day.
survey commissioned Pettitt conducted independent analyst Fraser Maywood found new energy projects WA - whether under construction or starting operation - had flatlined.
WA installed 1.2 gigawatts solar windfarms past 20 years. government' own modelling showed WA would need bring 50GW new large-scale renewables - or 63 times amount solar 16 times wind - 2042.
" abundantly clear ramp renewable energy scale needed government' own forecasts requires urgent dramatic shift government policy," report, obtained Guardian Australia, found.

slowdown South West Interconnected System project contrasted official government position there " large number renewable energy projects pipeline", said.
Industry representatives interviewed survey identified "significant challenges" such land network access state grid operations. they had " universal fear voicing these concerns may harm future prospects".
government spokesperson did respond questions modelling said state recognised " need take urgent action address climate change" committed achieving net zero emissions 2050.
"While our state' path net zero unique given economy supported hard--abate industries, WA well placed play critical role global energy transition renewable hydrogen critical minerals exporter," spokesperson said. "Our government' decision retire all state-owned coal-fired power plants 2030 play major part WA' decarbonisation efforts.
government had committed invest $3.8bn wind generation energy storage, said. If invested, emissions main grid would cut 40%.
Pettitt said there "real danger" renewables would progress time, resulting state instead commissioning gas-fired power plants.
Other states already picking between 20m 30m tonnes CO2-e because WA' outsized emissions. load swell even if WA used gas.
"These investments aren't short term nor emissions," Pettitt said.

vast majority environmental projects most frequently used offset greenhouse gas emissions appear fundamental failings suggesting they cannot relied upon cut planet-heating emissions, according new analysis.
global, multibillion-dollar voluntary carbon trading industry embraced governments, organisations corporations including oil gas companies, airlines, fast-food brands, fashion houses, tech firms, art galleries universities way claiming reduce greenhouse gas footprint.
works carbon offset credits being tradable "allowances" or certificates allows purchaser compensate 1 ton carbon dioxide or equivalent greenhouse gases investing environmental projects claim reduce carbon emissions.
there mounting evidence suggesting many these offset schemes exaggerate climate benefits underestimate potential harms.
new investigation, Guardian researchers Corporate Accountability, non-profit, transnational corporate watchdog, analysed top 50 emission offset projects, those sold most carbon credits global market.
According our criteria classification system:
* total 39 top 50 emission offset projects, or 78% them, categorised likely junk or worthless due one or fundamental failing undermines promised emission cuts.
* Eight others (16%) look problematic, evidence suggesting they may least one fundamental failing potentially junk, according classification system applied.
* efficacy remaining three projects (6%) determined definitively there insufficient public, independent information adequately assess quality credits /or accuracy claimed climate benefits.
* Overall, $1.16bn (£937m) carbon credits traded so far projects classified investigation likely junk or worthless; further $400m credits bought sold potentially junk.
50 most popular global projects include forestry schemes, hydroelectric dams, solar wind farms, waste disposal greener household appliances schemes across 20 (mostly) developing countries, according data AlliedOffsets, most comprehensive emissions trading database, tracks projects inception. They account almost third entire global voluntary carbon market (VCM), suggesting junk or overvalued carbon credits exaggerate emission reduction benefits norm.
our analysis, project classified likely junk if there compelling evidence, claims or high risk cannot guarantee additional, permanent greenhouse gas cuts among other criteria. some cases, there evidence suggesting project leak greenhouse gas emissions or shift emissions elsewhere. other cases, climate benefits appeared exaggerated or project would happened independently - or without voluntary carbon market.
come eve UN climate ambition summit New York amid mounting concerns world leaders make further climate pledges rely what non-industry experts increasingly refer flawed market-driven solutions rather justly rapidly transitioning off fossil fuels.
" ramifications analysis huge, points systemic failings voluntary market, providing additional evidence junk carbon credits pervade market," said Anuradha Mittal, director Oakland Institute thinktank. " cannot afford waste any time false solutions. issues far-reaching pervasive, extending well beyond specific verifiers. VCM actively exacerbating climate emergency."
certifiers/registries most projects top 50 said VCM important tool fighting climate crisis methodologies safeguards continually improving.
Independent experts say any chance being effective, carbon credits must tied new permanent emissions-reducing activities lead additional cuts would happened otherwise - should lead collateral damage environment or communities. Evidence overestimation how much carbon pollution project really saving, either intentionally or unintentionally, key determining whether project able guarantee promised benefits.
Our analysis builds previous independent investigations academic research into efficacy weaknesses VCM mitigating global heating, climate breakdown intensifies. draws information public private sources including academic studies, civil society research, offset project certifiers/registries, private sector databases ratings, media investigations. addition, assessed strength rigorousness available evidence took into account our grading each project.
Guardian/Corporate Accountability classification system assessed whether each offset project counted upon lead promised additional, permanent emission cuts - or. Assessing health, social, economic or other local benefits - or harms - beyond scope investigation did influence classification.
Strong evidence least one fundamental failing recorded means promised emission reductions - presumed primary objective VCM every offsets project - cannot guaranteed therefore classified likely junk or worthless.
According our research, third top 50 projects had some evidence three or fundamental failings. one case, giant forest conservation project Zimbabwe reported had so many exaggerated inflated claims - probably shifted emissions elsewhere - described "having financial holes Swiss cheese". Bloomberg reported rating experts who said project' emissions cuts overestimated five 30-fold.
US, most problematic project world' largest carbon capture storage plant Wyoming, benefited generous taxpayer subsidies, where vast majority captured CO2 released into atmosphere or sold other fossil fuel companies help extract hard--reach oil, according Institute Energy Economics Financial Analysis. certified American Carbon Registry (ACR), said new carbon credits issued 15 years - although corporations still retiring or purchasing old ones offset emissions.
Four carbon markets experts said findings based solid methodology shine light pitfalls market-driven climate solutions can enable polluters keep polluting instead transitioning off fossil fuels.
" heart carbon markets what seen many flawed notion avoiding or reducing emissions one place can make ongoing emissions elsewhere. Delaying transition [away fossil fuels] purchasing pollution credits only serves maintain business usual increase likelihood climate catastrophe," said Erika Lennon, senior attorney Center International Environmental Law (Ciel)' climate energy program.
Recent investigations found fundamental failings forest offset projects problems, according our research, appear widespread industry.
All 10 hydroelectric dams top 50 carbon offsets - including Teles Pires dam Brazilian Amazon Karcham Wangtoo plant Himalayas, India, both faced opposition tribal communities - classified likely junk our analysis climate benefits would happened anyway, regardless carbon credits. number previous studies shown although large dams cut national emissions, they do lead new or additional reductions needed effective carbon credits. some cases large dams associated land disputes, displacement, increased poverty environmental damage - including some greenhouse gas emissions emitted water reservoirs.
another example, program western Kenya provides water filters so households no longer need boil water firewood filters. Though potential health sanitation benefits questioned, emissions perspective classified likely junk our analysis due what appeared inflated baseline figures how many households used firewood heat water, exaggerated claims part due evidence low uptake filters.
Barbara Haya, director Berkeley Carbon Trading Project, recently published study concluded Verra' Redd+ forest schemes - like other major offset projects - exaggerate climate benefits, said: " overall [new] findings align research carbon credit quality finds widespread -crediting across many registries offset project types including those most credits market - avoided deforestation renewable energy."
response, Verra said many issues highlighted report would addressed forthcoming new methodology generating carbon credits.
Back 2021, International Energy Agency (IEA) warned there no further expansion oil, gas coal production if world wanted any chance avoiding catastrophic climate breakdown. world failed heed warning, emission cuts wildly off track, according recent UN global stocktake, most comprehensive analysis global climate action produced date.
" climate breakdown begun," said UN secretary general, António Guterres, after hottest atmosphere ocean temperatures recorded summer. "Our climate imploding faster can cope extreme weather events hitting every corner planet."
Scientists decades warning risks, yet instead phasing out fossil fuels, vast amounts time resources invested into market-based carbon offset schemes technologies trade, cap capture - rather cut - greenhouse gas emissions.
Estimated worth $2bn, voluntary carbon market (VCM) vast, fragmented opaque, involving complex network developers, registries, traders, brokers investors makes difficult track evaluate effectiveness - potential harms - linked offset projects.
Despite complexity, there strong public interest calculating benefits offset projects given deepening climate emergency lack meaningful progress reducing fossil fuel production global emissions.
Researchers Corporate Accountability created classification system analyse effectiveness top 50 offset projects, using raw data AlliedOffsets.
Most top 50 primarily promoted emission avoidance or reduction projects included handful carbon removals schemes. Carbon credits each project certified issued one seven registries; each project had verified third party.
* top 50 included three projects US, including tree-planting project Mississippi Delta 2020 Bloomberg investigation found took "credit trees already planted, or would planted anyway", oil company project Wyoming stores only fraction carbon captured. Both classified likely junk due evidence multiple fundamental failings. American Carbon Registry (ACR), certified both projects, rejected findings sources relied upon our analysis. ACR said tree planting project had enabled hundreds private landowners reforest 130,000 acres marginal degraded sites Mississippi Delta.
* Forty-seven top 50 projects Latin America, Africa Asia, predominantly countries contributed least climate crisis being battered effects. Five seven located Brazil classified investigation fundamentally flawed likely junk, while another problematic potentially junk. neighbouring Peru, all four forestry projects our top 50 evidence multiple failings making carbon credits likely worthless, according our analysis.
* nine offset projects India - five large hydroelectric dams, two solar plants two wind farms - all classified likely or potentially junk, part because none would lead additional emission reductions.
* One or fundamental flaws found 96% (22 out 23) forest offset projects located Brazil, Colombia, Uruguay, Indonesia, Ethiopia Zimbabwe among other countries.
* Fifteen 16 renewable energy projects, include dams, solar wind, likely junk - mostly because project would almost certainly happened anyway. While renewable energy essential energy transition, these carbon credit projects do appear leading claimed additional climate benefits should used polluters offset emissions.
"These findings show VCM flawed basically fraud, allowing west offset emissions continue business usual expense global south," said Souparna Lahiri, New Delhi-based climate adviser Global Forest Coalition.
" carbon market rich west, last 25 years actually done nothing mitigate climate crisis, rather, protected high-emitting industry, production, consumption lifestyle. It' story continuing widening inequity climate injustice," said Lahiri.
Almost two-thirds (32/50) most traded projects certified US-based non-profit Verra, operates world' most prominent carbon standard or registry, included 18 forest offsetting projects known Redd+ schemes.
Twenty-eight 32 Verra projects Guardian/Corporate Accountability analysed classified likely junk; other four problematic potentially worthless. Verra faced mounting criticism methodology lax safeguards including Guardian investigation found forest carbon offsets mostly junk make global heating worse.
Verra disputes criticism forest projects. "There compelling evidence proving effectiveness Redd+ projects," said Verra spokesperson who pointed recent research papers including 2022 study 40 Redd+ projects found deforestation degradation declined first five years implementation.
Verra did comment specific findings investigation, statement said: "Verra' role ensure projects satisfy program rules, such they can registered receive issuance credits. Project proponents free hold, sell or retire those credits thereafter discretion... Verra happy engage data, reconsider assumptions consider future improvements based sound feedback expert input."
Our analysis found similar issues offset projects other certifiers.
clean development mechanism (CDM), UN-led program first international carbon trading scheme, five eight offset projects classified likely junk - including Teles Pires hydropower dam Brazil reportedly damaged Indigenous traditional communities, harmed biodiversity fisheries, probably emitting significant carbon emissions.
CDM established part Kyoto protocol allows countries support emissions-reductions projects other nations while counting them toward own international climate commitments, follows "comprehensive set criteria selecting projects", UN framework convention climate change (UNFCCC) spokesperson said. Despite goals, CDM faced numerous criticisms exaggerated claims, lack community safeguards. One study found most certified renewable energy projects did provide real, measurable additional emission reductions.
spokesperson added: " supervisory body currently working creation new mechanism [certifier], striving make being highest possible integrity overcome any weaknesses pointed out past."
UNFCCC did respond growing concerns sustainable development mechanism (SDM), replacing CDM, already come under fire civil society groups across world promoting activities do lead emissions reductions risking harm local communities ecosystems, among other things.
Karcham Wangtoo hydroelectric plant Himachal Pradesh, India. Experts say large dams do lead additional emission cuts. Photograph: Rupendra Rawat/Alamy
another case, two out four projects Swiss-based Gold Standard Registry (GSR) - water filter program Kenya, cooking stove initiative Ghana promises cut emissions deforestation - classified likely junk or worthless carbon credits perspective. additional emission cuts claimed unlikely due exaggerated claims inflated baselines, analysis found.
GSR vehemently rejected methodology research disputed basis findings, saying company therefore comment conclusions projects. " pay close attention new academic research best practices update our methodologies ensure they take account leading science," said Jamie Ballantyne GSR. "Despite, inevitable mistakes made - why market-leading grievance process allows anyone submit grievance against any project. What can't do demand changes individual projects based upon inconsistent evidence every time any allegation made."

Mary Grady, executive director ACR, said: "Findings 'inflated baseline', 'exaggerated claims', ' additional' should reconsidered." She said natural tree regeneration accounted would skew baseline or benefit calculations; project proponent legally required monitor projects least 40 years.
past, amid mounting critical evidence bad publicity some corporations said they looking create own emission offset schemes - experts warn even harder track.
Carbon offsets continue promoted source climate financing global south, recent Africa Climate Summit, hundreds millions dollars pledged boost continent' carbon credit production.
Barbara Haya, director Berkeley Carbon Trading Project, said: " clear most important climate mitigation approach direct emissions reductions. Beyond, offset market reliable way support climate mitigation."

yearly survey Investor Group Climate Change investors asked energy climate solutions they believed had good long-term returns. Nuclear energy ranked last 14 possible responses, along sustainable oceans.
" due nuclear energy' very high cost, lack maturity deployment next generation technologies," policy brief survey said, citing CSIRO' gencost report.
five most popular options : renewable energy (backed 47% respondents); nature solutions, including biodiversity or nature capital (34%); energy storage (32%); low carbon transport (32%); industry/materials, including critical minerals (32%).
2021 70% investors cited policy regulatory uncertainty barrier investing climate solutions, figure dropped 40% 2023 data released Monday.
Asked nominate policies they wanted government prioritise, most investors (56%) called sector--sector decarbonisation plans keep global heating under 1.5C threshold.
There majority support improved carbon pricing through safeguard mechanism (54%), funding support new technology (53%), phasing out fossil fuel subsidies (51%).
policy brief said "emerging priorities" included mandatory climate-related disclosures, timelines phase-out coal, oil gas, clear policies build resilience adapt physical damages climate change.
Erwin Jackson, Investor Group Climate Change' managing director policy, said: "Investors given government pretty good report card.
"They're sending message credible, investible durable policy frameworks put place today support strong investor beneficiary returns into future, enhance Australia' economic competitiveness, help attract international capital.
" must fair fast transition net zero emissions, must adapt.
"Investors look forward working governments initiatives can incentivise remove barriers private sector investment adaptation across economy."

" whole--nation opportunity demands whole--economy approach."

Australia' main power grid faces "real" possibility ageing coal-fired power plants drop out before sufficient generation capacity transmission lines place, Australian Energy Market Operator said.
comments, contained draft report Aemo' main blueprint national electricity market, come day after New South Wales faced first grid strains summer. Authorities called consumers reduce non-essential power use temperatures hovered high-30s across much Sydney.
Aemo' report, known integrated system plan, noted 10 big coal-fired power plants had shut national electricity market since 2012. remaining coal fleet may shut much three times faster companies flagged own public announcements.
Coal-fired power generation capacity. Photograph: Australian Energy Market Operator
Aemo said mostly likely scenario 90% current 21 gigawatts coal capacity would retire 2034-35 all 2038. Even "progressive change" path, only 4GW coal generation would remain 2034-35.
scale replacement capacity needed huge. Solar windfarms need triple 2030 57GW expand seven-fold 2050 126GW.
support variable renewables, new storage, hydro- gas-powered generation need increase four-fold 74GW 2050. Roof-top solar, already installed higher percentage houses, need expand four times mid-century 72W, Aemo said.
"While significant progress [ transition off fossil fuels] being made, challenges risks already being experienced," Aemo' report said. "Planned projects progressing expected, due approval processes, investment decision uncertainty, cost pressures, social licence issues, supply chain issues workforce shortages."
Under two three main scenarios examined, grid need extra 10,000km transmission 2050. report said if Australia pursues policy developing so-called green energy exports, such hydrogen, twice transmission construction needed.
energy industry itself calling acceleration new investments if Australia meet 2030 decarbonisation goals - ensure power supplies can meet demand. New investment decisions dwindling, despite urgency, some firms complaining changing rules states such NSW.
federal government' plan capacity investment scheme provide minimum prices much 32GW new renewables storage aimed accelerate new projects raised concerns some private companies excessive state involvement market.
draft integrated system plan may odds some firms' plans. Victoria, instance, may closed coal plants entirely 2033, Aemo predicts, while AGL Energy says Loy Yang power station shut end 2035 financial.
" identify optimal development path 2050, used Australia' most comprehensive set power system market models assess benefits risks 1,000 potential pathways," Aemo' chief executive, Daniel Westerman, said.
"Delivering transmission projects identified plan expected avoid $17bn additional costs consumers if those projects delivered," said.
Separately, NSW government Friday released design proposed new plan aims bolster energy reliability coal-fired plants close.
If applied, national orderly exit management framework would provide " clear process governments manage situations where owners coal-fired power station seek bring forward retirement date", government said. "If required, enables government temporarily extend operation power station while new renewable infrastructure comes online, through voluntary agreement or direction," said.
NSW' energy minister, Penny Sharpe, said government "committed getting much renewable energy into our grid quickly possible meet our emissions reduction targets provide reliable supply clean, affordable electricity".
" don't want coal-fired power stations open minute longer needed," Sharpe said. " framework provides back- energy transition, used only last resort where don't enough time feasibly get new renewables or storage into system."

100 heads state government expected land Baku, capital Azerbaijan, next few days first thing they likely notice smell oil. odour hangs heavy air, evidence abundance fossil fuels small country shores Caspian Sea.
Flaring refineries lights night sky, city dotted diminutive "nodding donkey" oil wells raising lowering pistons they draw earth. Even national symbol gas flame, epitomised shape three skyscrapers tower city.
Azerbaijan built oil since mid-19th century, fossil fuels now make 90% exports. There no starker reminder core question world leaders come Baku decide: whether planet burn so fossil fuel producers can continue make money, or whether take different path.
world' biggest economy, US, shift away focus clean energy fostered Joe Biden towards "drill, baby, drill" policies Donald Trump main topic conversation tens thousands delegates Cop29 UN climate summit. However, many point out no country ever produced much oil gas US does now, 20% oil gas licences issued during Biden administration during Trump' first term.
Climate leaders reacted defiance US election outcome. " result election seen major blow global climate action cannot halt changes under way decarbonise economy meet goals Paris agreement," declared Christiana Figueres, former UN climate chief who co-founder Global Optimism thinktank.
Trump Cop29, fortnight-long meeting latest near annual series stretching back 1992 when UN framework convention climate change - parent treaty 2015 Paris climate agreement - signed.
Those talks may appear achieved little, greenhouse gas emissions still rising losses extreme weather - record hurricanes Atlantic, dramatic floods last week Spain, drought Africa threatened millions famine - becoming daily apparent. Last hottest record likely hotter still.
Just 15 years ago, however, matters looked much worse. Renewable energy then expensive scarcely used, world headed 6C global heating above preindustrial temperatures, level would barely support human life. Today, after years talks - during fossil fuel interests repeatedly spread disinformation, blocked agreement, captured politicians choked investment renewables - heading "only" 3C.
Global heating scale would still devastating, scientists made clear. So countries meeting Cop29 Monday reaffirm commitments limiting temperature rises 1.5C, require reaching net zero emissions next two decades. Scientists say there still chance avoiding worst ravages climate breakdown if world acts now.
" reality remains unless world collectively steps efforts, impacts climate change become increasingly severe frequent felt increasing number people all countries, including US," said Kaveh Guilanpour, vice-president international strategies Center Climate Energy Solutions, US thinktank. " reality there no prosperous or safe high-carbon future anyone."
prospects strong outcome Baku summit may appear dim, far right anti-net zero voices rise US, Europe elsewhere. there hope Cop29 address least one major issues preventing action: money. Shifting clean energy makes economic well scientific sense, incumbency fossil fuels so strong massive investment needed shift world lower-carbon, lower-waste, productive healthier path.
How much? $3.5tn between now 2050 transform energy systems, according Energy Transitions Commission thinktank. UN, through climate champions network, estimates $125tn total 2050, or $5tn, whole global economy. McKinsey report two years ago put total $9tn, analysis debunked using too-high cost estimates ignoring existing investments.
Where get money? Most already there. Trillions dollars sounds like lot world already spends $3tn energy, according International Energy Agency, other industries fossil fuel-based economy. If investment geared away high-carbon infrastructure towards cleaner alternatives, sums easily met.
developed countries, all needed achieve switch strong governance directs investment away dirty infrastructure into low-carbon technologies. tricky enough - witness election Trump clear anti-net zero ticket. developing world problem looks much harder. Poor countries already face high barriers gaining investment they need lift people out poverty, develop industries fund basic services.
instance, Africa most abundant resources solar power wind energy. data International Energy Agency last showed there solar panels Belgium whole African continent. Setting solar wind farms Africa far costly rich countries, despite lower physical labour costs, because investors charge far lend capital demand higher repayments.
same time, impacts climate breakdown wreaking havoc poor economies, frequently wiping out any development gains. Tinaye Mabara, Botswana-based activist Zimbabwe, said people suffering consequences. "Southern Africa, where I'm, bottom tier African regions receive climate finance, so I want see drastic climate finance reforms taken Cop ensure local adaptation efforts actually receive necessary funding, because they ones ground doing work."
Daniel Lund, senior adviser Fijian government, said developing countries urgently needed help, greater quantity climate finance through better systems make easier access. "Addressing complex impacts climate change through existing fragmented volatile climate financing arrangements can feel like fighting raging fire dozens tiny fire extinguishers," said. "None tools currently available sufficient scale problem."
why money core focus Cop29. Under Paris agreement, countries must agree "new collective quantified goal" (NCQG) climate finance, flow rich poor, help developing countries cut greenhouse gas emissions adapt impacts climate crisis.
something never tried before three decades talks. Until now, only climate finance target promise made 2009, Cop15 Copenhagen, $100bn flow developing world 2020. target only achieved two years late - delay damaged faith developing countries, according Alok Sharma, president Cop26 Glasgow now member UK' House Lords. " reaching previous $100bn goal time sapped confidence trust developing nations, that' why any future finance goal meet deliverability test," said.
No Cop ever discussed finance goal before, let alone any tested rationale. $100bn figure "plucked out air", said Rachel Kyte, UK' new climate envoy, speaking before her appointment role. offer made - largely instigation then US secretary state, Hillary Clinton - based political calculations what developed country governments thought electorates would stand.
Cop29, rigorous systems place. Two years ago economists Nicholas Stern Vera Songwe led -depth analysis climate finance needs. They came number: $2.4tn needed developing countries, excluding China, cut greenhouse gas emissions protect themselves against climate breakdown.
sum, half come countries' existing budgets domestic private investment sources, economists estimated. would leave roughly $1tn come developed world, large chunk Lord Stern believes come reforming World Bank fellow institutions, enable them lend, easier terms, climate-related efforts.
Most analysts developed country governments agree these estimates. Most developing countries broadly accept them, stance reflected proposals NCQG they submitted UN. example, India submitted demand $1tn, Alliance Small Island States targeting similar number, while Africa group wants $1.3tn.
these figures statement need. Few believe all money come exchequers developed countries without risking backlash. Joe Thwaites, NRDC thinktank US, said: "There keen awareness fragility political consensus."
Azerbaijan presidency, clear early talks reconciling these two approaches - one based need, other what developed countries prepared stump - means including least two key numbers NCQG. What emerged pre-Cop talks recent months "layered approach". These layers led some participants call onion, others camembert (because sources cash divided into wedges), while few prefer comparisons pie or sandwich.
Culinary metaphors aside, core concept fairly simple: NCQG include needs expressed developing countries; agreed goal finance coming existing developed countries plus expanded contributor base; ideas how fill gap between two.
No developed countries yet prepared specific how much "quantum" contributions should, goal collective one, so individual countries need submit own targets. Guardian understands overall sums region $400bn $600bn 2035 being discussed. Much if most funding likely come directly developed world taxpayers through multilateral development banks such World Bank International Monetary Fund, require sweeping reform before they capable meeting challenge.
order agree such sums, rich countries demanding redrawing donor terms expand list contributors. global economy changed drastically since 1992 when UNFCCC signed, divided world starkly into developed countries obligations cut emissions provide finance, developing countries carried no such obligations. China now biggest economy biggest emitter no obligation offer climate finance, other middle-income countries such South Korea Singapore booming. Petrostates such Saudi Arabia, Qatar United Arab Emirates classed developing, despite vast oil wealth.
China agree developing country status changed insists already provides de facto climate finance form loans poorer countries. There may compromise available counting some these forms finance, voluntary basis, towards expanded donor base. UAE Azerbaijan made gestures, pledging relatively small quantities. Saudi Arabia, however, firmly set against being tapped donor.
bridge gap hundreds billions dollars, countries pinning hopes what being termed "innovative sources finance". These include potential taxes high-carbon activities, such oil gas extraction; wealth taxes, such billionaire tax proposed Brazil; levies frequent flyers global shipping; proceeds sales carbon credits; redirecting harmful subsidies fossil fuels agriculture towards sustainable options.
US under Biden lukewarm some these ideas - Treasury secretary, Janet Yellen, said firm no any wealth tax, instance - least participant. Under Trump, US may try stymie any prospect global levies.
Yet impact Trump government NCQG likely smaller feared. US provided just $1.5bn climate finance 2021, $5.8bn 2022 $9.5bn 2023. These sums paltry compared US' historical responsibilities carbon emitter, according activists. EU bigger provider climate finance US.
Alden Meyer, climate change thinktank E3G, said: " US contribution what should, under any government. There some impact much."
Answering money question would huge boost climate action, climate justice world' poorest most vulnerable people. enough make Cop29 success. Current plans national governments cut greenhouse gas emissions - called nationally determined contributions (NDCs) - inadequate urgently need strengthened.
Under Paris agreement, next round NDCs due February, before Cop30 conference held Brazil next November. Some countries file theirs early - UK promised announce plans Cop29 - Brazil wants use summit Baku ensure countries come NDCs strong, detailed contain targets commensurate task reaching net zero mid-century.

Developing countries should amplify diplomatic economic pressure, underscoring global interdependence holding wealthier nations accountable historical emissions responsibilities."
Owing Trump' victory, these efforts may seem futile. Simon Lewis, professor global change science University College London, spoke many scientists saying: " can say goodbye Paris agreement goal limiting warming 1.5C above preindustrial levels. Increasingly deadly climate impacts escalate. It' simple."
Activists governments ready give yet. Abandoning 1.5C target would give too much ground those - including Saudi Arabia allies - who already argue retreat much easier 2C limit set down 2015 Paris agreement. Yet scientists warned rise 2C would disastrous, small islands particular, bringing droughts floods, would risk triggering calamitous tipping points - such collapse Atlantic meridional overturning circulation brings warm weather northern Europe.
Rather do, Brazil - backed developed countries including UK, EU, developing countries including members Alliance Small Island States - keep focus 1.5C push all countries - even US - come NDCs achieve.
