Renewable energy generation hit new record Friday, briefly contributing two-thirds power Australia' main grid.
According Australian Energy Market Operator (Aemo), milestone set 12.30pm, contribution 68.7%, or 18,882MW, renewable sources.
figure 4.6 percentage points higher previous record, set 18 September.
total power grid Friday, 34% came distributed solar, outstripped black coal' contribution 22%.
Renewable penetration rates measured 30-minute intervals, illustrate contributions grid within short period time.
"It' very different 100% renewables 24/7," said Alison Reeve, climate change energy deputy program director Grattan Institute. "Nevertheless, does show how much grid changing."
"Five years ago maximum we'd managed get 30%, five years before, I don't know anyone even measuring [renewables], so small."
One challenge energy transition managing fluctuating contributions renewable sources, Reeve said. "Once solar dropped out [ night], percentage you need ramp your non-renewables lot higher," she said.
"One things gradually driving particularly coal-fired power plants out market moment they can't ramp down quickly... they're good switching off couple hour periods."
Gas hydro generators responsive short timescales. Because high gas prices currently, "when those gas generators come they set quite high price electricity market," Reeve said.
Hydro generators recently limited operation because wet weather east coast Australia, she added. "They can't send too much water down river because they don't want make any flooding worse," she said.

Another challenge replacing "system' stability" coal gas provide electricity grid - ability drop raise generation slightly order "keep voltage grid balanced", Reeve said, require long-duration storage infrastructure such batteries.
"Until figure out way get balancing role done other things like pumped hydro batteries, enough those system, there natural upper limit how much renewables penetration, particularly once you move beyond instantaneous... start talk what can sustain four or eight hours."
Reeve described these "solvable problems", required ironing out details including costs, storage location how services would valued.
July report International Renewable Energy Agency (Irena) found Australia now among world leaders cheap solar energy.
Behind China India, 2021 Australia had third-lowest utility-scale solar cost world, $0.042 USD/kWh (AU$0.065). represented 21% -- drop price.
According Irena data, average cost electricity utility-scale solar dropped 90% Australia since 2010.

uptake these energy-saving measures depends heavily government policy, according analysis Cambridge Econometrics, commissioned Greenpeace.

economic boost forecast Cambridge Econometrics analysis, entitled Economic Impacts Decarbonising Heating Residential Buildings, published Greenpeace Tuesday, comes mainly savings soaring energy bills creation green jobs, positive impact rest economy freeing people' spending.
There health social benefits, people living under-heated homes prone illness, while lifting people out fuel poverty improves wellbeing education prospects children.

Global public subsidies fossil fuels almost doubled $700bn 2021, analysis shown, representing "roadblock" tackling climate crisis.
Despite huge profits fossil fuel companies, subsidies soared governments sought shield citizens surging energy prices global economy rebounded Covid-19 pandemic.
Most subsidies used reduce price paid consumers. largely benefits wealthier households, they use most energy, rather targeting those low incomes. subsidies expected rise even further 2022 Russia' war Ukraine driven energy prices even higher.
"Fossil fuel subsidies roadblock sustainable future, difficulty governments face removing them underscored times high volatile fuel prices," said Fatih Birol, director International Energy Agency, produced analysis OECD.
" surge investment clean energy technologies infrastructure only lasting solution today' global energy crisis best way reduce exposure consumers high fuel costs," said Birol.
"Significant increases fossil fuel subsidies encourage wasteful consumption, while necessarily reaching low-income households," said Mathias Cormann, OECD secretary general. " need adopt measures protect consumers [] help keep us track carbon neutrality, well energy security affordability."
analysis covers 51 key countries represents 85% world' total energy supply. Subsidies kept fossil fuel prices artificially low tripled $531bn 2021, compared 2020. Subsidies oil gas production reached record level $64bn. IEA said May 2021 no new fossil fuel projects should developed if world meet climate goals.
" period soaring fossil fuel energy prices, when oil gas companies posting record-breaking profits, should ideal time governments eliminate fossil fuel production subsidies - so see rising government support fossil fuels now infuriating," said Gyorgy Dallos, Greenpeace International.
"Governments need stick green pledges. consumer side, they urgently need replace untargeted support measures targeted income support people experiencing fuel poverty."
G20 agreed 2009 phase out "inefficient" fossil fuel subsidies, 2016, G7 set deadline 2025. little progress made.
$697bn total covers explicit subsidies, including price reductions, government funding tax breaks. Estimates including implicit subsidies, ie cost climate air pollution damage caused fossil fuels, far higher. These amounted $5.9tn 2020, according International Monetary Fund, or $11m minute.
Guardian revealed July oil gas sector made average $1tn pure profit last 50 years. These vast sums provided power "buy every politician, every system" delay action climate crisis, said Prof Aviel Verbruggen, author analysis.
energy crisis proving extremely profitable oil companies, even without subsidies. first six months 2022, five leading companies - BP, Shell, ExxonMobil, Chevron Total - made adjusted profits nearly $100bn. figure same sum rich nations had promised poor nations help deal climate crisis 2020, failed deliver.

Germany saving less gas necessary rule out shortages winter, head German grid agency said, country missed crucial target 20% cut consumption last week amid dropping temperatures.
Germany, used draw half natural gas needs Russia before start Ukraine war, scrambled open new sources energy during last ten months.
Nonetheless, summer, economy minister, Robert Habeck, announced target overall savings 20% across Germany, higher 15% goal set European Commission.
After relatively mild autumn during German businesses households saved gas required emergency plan, storage tanks rose almost 100% result, cold snap end last week rung alarm bells.
"Currently, total savings only 13%," Klaus Müller, head federal network agency utilities, Bundesnetzagentur, told Tagesspiegel newspaper Monday.
"If remains outlier, need worry yet. remain cold next few days," said. " temperatures -10C [14F], gas consumption shoots dramatically."
now, Germany' gas storage tanks remain 95% full, country planning ceremonially unveil first terminal importing liquefied natural gas (LNG) North Sea port Wilhelmshaven Saturday.
" now getting gas several sources," Müller said. " soon three terminals liquid gas, getting good deliveries Norway, Holland, Belgium via France."
dismissed possibility households switching electric heaters overloading grid. During cold snaps November people had " stupid expensive idea heating homes electricity instead gas," Müller said. "Luckily no one did."
stoppage Russia gas deliveries looking permanent after explosions Nord Stream 1 2 pipelines September, however, there continues nervous uncertainty Berlin gas rationing winter 2023-24, well possibility acts sabotage other areas critical energy infrastructure.
If German government announce gas emergency, Müller' agency would choice between rationing gas supply entire German regions, individual companies especially high demands, or sectors industry declares non-vital.
interview Guardian last month, Müller said meeting 20% savings target would require "unrelenting effort", stressed importance governments urging citizens make savings.
"I think it' important governments come clean citizens: without significant reduction our gas use won't get through winter without problems," Müller said. "Campaigns urging consumers make such savings right thing do, because they can empowering. Keeping quiet risks, or denying them, strikes me wrong strategy."

Australian electricity providers ranked climate credentials new consumer guide aims cut through "greenwash".
Greenpeace' 2022 Green Electricity Guide, released Tuesday, assessed 48 Australian energy retailers six main criteria: providing renewable energy, ending coal use 2030, halting fossil fuel expansion, supporting new renewable energy, transparency marketing pollution environmental harm. Then, each provider given score out five stars ranked.
first time guide updated since 2018. Glenn Walker, senior energy campaigner Greenpeace author 2022 guide says researchers placed emphasis "cutting through greenwash spin" when producing update. included reviewing provider websites marketing materials "assess how upfront they where energy comes ".

Australia' energy giants make bottom ranks. "They worst polluters, companies people should switching away," Walker says.

secretive investor court system poses real threat Paris climate agreement, activists said, governments taking action phase out fossil fuels face slew multimillion-dollar lawsuits lost profits.
New data seen Guardian shows surge cases under energy charter treaty (ECT), obscure international agreement allows energy corporations sue governments policies hurt profits.
Coal oil investors already suing governments several billions compensation lost profits energy policy changes. example, German energy company RWE suing Netherlands €1.4bn (£1.2bn) plans phase out coal, while Rockhopper Exploration, based UK, suing Italian government after banned new drilling near coast.
"It' real threat [ Paris agreement]. It' biggest threat I am aware," said Yamina Saheb, former employee ECT secretariat who quit 2018 raise alarm.
" Paris agreement... means need decarbonise current decade before 2030," said Saheb, co-author Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change' report mitigation. She estimated foreign investors sue governments €1.3tn until 2050 compensation early closure coal, oil gas plants - sum exceeds what EU hopes spend green deal next decade.
compensation companies paid public funds, governments would less money pay new technology make buildings, transport industry greener. Saheb argued these payments endanger green transition. "It' impossible do everything," she said.
Separate analysis treaty shared exclusively Guardian showed 269% increase cases 2011-20 compared previous decade. " going see future many cases," said Lucí Bárcena, Amsterdam-based Transnational Institute, who compiled data. Since 2013, two-thirds cases brought against western European governments.
" energy charter treaty... no cohesion all [EU] climate policies," Bárcena said. "Trade investment agreements binding states, means if they break contract then they pay huge amounts money, while there no other mechanism binds countries goals they setting Cop26. There big asymmetry."
Representatives ECT' 54 members meeting next week ninth round talks modernising treaty.
Signed 1994, treaty intended protect western companies investing oil- gas-rich countries former Soviet Union. Only foreign investors, rather domestic ones, can use system, prompting longstanding complaints privileged access. Campaigners now fear stymie green transition.
" obviously get out fossil field quite quickly energy charter treaty way because protects fossil fuels," said Cornelia Maarfield, senior trade investment policy coordinator Climate Action Network Europe. "Our main concern once governments start taking decisions phase out coal, gas oil, they run into difficulties investment protection chapter agreement."
Germany' RWE suing Netherlands €1.4bn after Dutch government decided close all coal-fired power plants 2030, including RWE' Eemshaven plant, began operating 2015 expected life-span 40 years. RWE said supported energy transition Netherlands, " only issue therefore fact coal ban law does provide adequate compensation".
Another German utility, Uniper, reported seeking between €850m €1bn early closure Maasvlakte coal-fired power plant near Rotterdam, opened 2016. company declined confirm damages seeking, saying: "Uniper convinced shutting down our power plant Maasvlakte after only 15 years operation would unlawful without adequate compensation."
London-listed Rockhopper suing Italy after MPs 2016 reintroduced restrictions drilling oil gas within 12 nautical miles coast. Rockhopper, never revealed size claim, said September suing "very significant monetary damages basis lost profits" after Italian government rejected oil exploration plans Ombrina Mare project Adriatic Sea.
Rockhopper said: " Italian government issued licences encouraged significant investment oil gas exploration based platform. Clearly equitable change rules halfway through. important note Italy continues produce significant quantities oil gas within 12 miles coast."
Investors known filed 142 cases against governments since ECT came into force 1998. these only known cases. Even ECT' Brussels-based secretariat acknowledges does complete picture, because investors obliged reveal legal action under ECT.
Urban Rusnák, secretary general Energy Charter Secretariat, rejected view treaty would hamper governments getting out fossil fuels, saying did proscribe energy policy. " treaty does ban governments [ stopping fossil fuels] There nothing like automatic punishment."
suggested governments quit fossil fuels without disputes under ECT if they took smart approach. "If government clever enough they care investment climate, they can manage around," said.
Rusnák, who grew communist Czechoslovakia, stressed importance ECT upholding rule law. "Some recent requests climate activists ban all fossil fuels without any compensation coming very close what happened Czechoslovakia... when regime 1948 decided expropriate all industries good reason social justice," said. "My point here individual cases. It' system. Either western civilisation really believe what agreed: honour our agreements, if conditions change sit down negotiate how get out."
Neither did agree proposal NGOs EU governments should quit ECT en masse. " legally possible consequences dire," said, referring 20- sunset clause meant signatories remained bound two decades. "Please serious reform... Otherwise you locked treaty you don't like 20 years."
Saheb argues treaty beyond reform because central Asian member states veto any weakening protection fossil fuels. " EU countries should withdraw altogether one," she said. "If withdraw altogether agree cancel clause then move our energy transition."

dash gas comes scientists warned "now or never" climate. Global greenhouse gas emissions must halved 2030, according Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change, give world chance limiting global temperature rises 1.5C above pre-industrial levels, target agreed last year' Cop26 climate summit.

Separate research found moving directly renewable energy coal cheaper using gas "transition" fuel.
Bill Hare, chief executive Climate Analytics, partner Climate Action Tracker, said world compounding mistake made after Covid-19 pandemic struck, when despite soaring green rhetoric, few countries made decisive shift favour low-carbon economic recovery.
warned: "Something change: cannot go responding short-term shocks, they pandemics or energy shocks conflict, taking steps would increase emissions, ignoring looming crisis climate change."
Hare pointed many alternative policies governments should use, such improving energy efficiency, ramping renewable energy, boosting public transport imposing windfall taxes bumper profits fossil fuel companies. These largely neglected many countries, CAT report warned, focused short-term energy supply responses instead.

Accusations greenwashing against major oil companies claim transition clean energy well-founded, according most comprehensive study date.
research, published peer-reviewed scientific journal, examined records ExxonMobil, Chevron, Shell BP, together responsible 10% global carbon emissions since 1965. researchers analysed data 12 years 2020 concluded company claims do align actions, include increasing rather decreasing exploration.
study found sharp rise mentions "climate", "low-carbon" "transition" annual reports recent years, especially Shell BP, increasing pledges action strategies. concrete actions rare researchers said: "Financial analysis reveals continuing business model dependence fossil fuels along insignificant opaque spending clean energy."
Numerous previous studies shown there already reserves oil gas planned production burned while keeping below internationally agreed temperature target 1.5C. May 2021, International Energy Agency (IEA) said there can no new fossil fuel developments if world reach net zero 2050.
Oil companies under increasing pressure investors align businesses climate targets. plans faced scepticism, prompting researchers conduct new research, they said objective comprehensive.
"Until there very concrete progress, every reason very sceptical claims moving green direction," said Prof Gregory Trencher, Kyoto University Japan, who worked Mei Li Jusen Asuka Tohoku University.
"If they moving away fossil fuels would expect see, example, declines exploration activity, fossil fuel production, sales profit fossil fuels," said. " if anything, find evidence reverse happening."
"Recent pledges look very nice they're getting lot people excited, put these context company history actions," Trencher said. "It' like very naughty schoolboy telling teacher 'I promise do all my homework next week', student never worked hard."
new study, published journal PLOS One, found mentions climate-related keywords annual reports rose sharply 2009 2020. example, BP' use "climate change" went 22 326 mentions.
terms strategy actions, researchers found " companies pledging transition clean energy setting targets they making concrete actions".
Chevron ExxonMobil "laggards" compared Shell BP, researchers said, even European majors' actions appeared contradict pledges. example, BP Shell pledged reduce investments fossil fuel extraction projects, both increased acreage new exploration recent years, researchers said.
Furthermore, analysis found Shell, BP, Chevron increased fossil fuel production volumes study period. None companies directly releases data investments clean energy, information they provided Carbon Disclosure Project indicates low average levels ranging 0.2% ExxonMobil 2.3% BP annual capital expenditure (capex). Separate analysis IEA indicates investment clean energy oil gas companies 1% capex 2020.
"Until actions investment behaviour brought into alignment discourse, accusations greenwashing appear well-founded," researchers said.
spokesperson ExxonMobil said: " move lower emission future requires multiple solutions can implemented scale. plan play leading role energy transition, while retaining investment flexibility across portfolio evolving opportunities, including example carbon capture, hydrogen biofuels, maximise shareholder returns."
Chevron spokesperson said: " focused lowering carbon intensity our operations seeking grow lower carbon businesses along our traditional business lines. planning $10bn lower carbon investments 2028."
Shell' spokesperson said: "Shell' target become net zero emissions energy business 2050, step society. Our short, medium long term intensity absolute targets consistent ambitious 1.5C goal Paris Agreement. first energy company submit energy transition strategy shareholders vote, securing strong endorsement."
spokesperson BP said: " 2020 BP set out our new net zero ambition, aims strategy, 2021 completed largest transformation company our history deliver these. Because paper looks back historically period 2009-2020, don't believe take these developments our progress fully into account."
Trencher rejected charge analysis out date: " included documents published during 2021, so so-called data gap only six months don't find any evidence any new actions would change any our findings."
"Unfortunately, way energy markets structured around world, fossil fuels still enjoy many [regulatory tax] advantages renewables still disadvantaged," said.

fossil fuel industry benefits subsidies $11m every minute, according analysis International Monetary Fund.
IMF found production burning coal, oil gas subsidised $5.9tn 2020, single country pricing all fuels sufficiently reflect full supply environmental costs. Experts said subsidies "adding fuel fire" climate crisis, time when rapid reductions carbon emissions urgently needed.
Explicit subsidies cut fuel prices accounted 8% total tax breaks another 6%. biggest factors failing make polluters pay deaths poor health caused air pollution (42%) heatwaves other impacts global heating (29%).
Setting fossil fuel prices reflect true cost would cut global CO2 emissions third, IMF analysts said. would big step towards meeting internationally agreed 1.5C target. Keeping target within reach key goal UN Cop26 climate summit November.
Agreeing rules carbon markets, enable proper pricing pollution, another Cop26 goal. "Fossil fuel price reform timelier," IMF researchers said. ending fossil fuel subsidies would prevent nearly million deaths dirty air raise trillions dollars governments, they said.
"There would enormous benefits reform, so there' enormous amount stake," said Ian Parry, lead author IMF report. "Some countries reluctant raise energy prices because they think harm poor. holding down fossil fuel prices highly inefficient way help poor, because most benefits accrue wealthier households. would better target resources towards helping poor vulnerable people directly."
50 countries committed net zero emissions mid-century 60 carbon pricing schemes around world, there some encouraging signs, Parry said: " still just scratching surface really, there' awful long way go."
G20 agreed 2009 phase out "inefficient" fossil fuel subsidies 2016, G7 set deadline 2025, little progress made. July, report showed G20 countries had subsidised fossil fuels trillions dollars since 2015, Paris climate deal reached.
" stabilise global temperatures must urgently move away fossil fuels instead adding fuel fire," said Mike Coffin, senior analyst thinktank Carbon Tracker. "It' critical governments stop propping industry decline, look accelerate low-carbon energy transition, our future, instead.
" host Cop26, UK government play important global leadership role ending all subsidies fossil fuels, well halting new North Sea licensing rounds," said. International Energy Agency (IEA) said May development new oil gas fields must stop meet climate goals.
comprehensive IMF report found prices least 50% below true costs 99% coal, 52% diesel 47% natural gas 2020. Five countries responsible two-thirds subsidies: China, US, Russia, India Japan. Without action, subsidies rise $6.4tn 2025, IMF said.
Proper pricing fossil fuels would cut emissions, example, encouraging electricity generators switch coal renewable energy making electric cars even cheaper option motorists. International cooperation important, Parry said, allay fears countries lose competitiveness if fossil fuel prices higher.
" IMF report sobering reading, pointing one major defects global economy," said Maria Pastukhova, thinktank e3g. " IEA' net-zero roadmap projects $5tn necessary 2030 put world pathway climate-safe world. maddening realise much-needed change start happening now, if governments' entanglement fossil fuels industry so many major economies."
"Fossil fuel subsidies major stumbling block G20 process years," she said. "Now all eyes G20 leaders' summit late October."
Ipek Gençü, Overseas Development Institute, said: "[Subsidy reform] requires support vulnerable consumers who impacted rising costs, well workers industries simply shut down. requires information campaigns, showing how savings redistributed society form healthcare, education other social services. Many people oppose subsidy reform because they see solely governments taking something away, giving back."
G20 countries emit almost 80% global greenhouse gases. 600 global companies Mean Business coalition, including Unilever, Ikea, Aviva, Siemens Volvo Cars, recently urged G20 leaders end fossil fuel subsidies 2025.

Households save £400 energy bills if clocks put back end October, according expert, who said would help people cost living crisis reduce pressure National Grid winter.
Evening energy demand peaks between 5pm 7pm during winter. If clocks stayed daylight saving time (DST), would remain light least part time, reducing carbon emissions energy demand.
Prof Aoife Foley, clean energy expert Queen' University Belfast, said: " simply forgoing winter DST October, save energy because brighter evening during winter, so reduce commercial residential electrical demand people leave work earlier, go home earlier, meaning less lighting heating needed."
would help government tackle "energy war" Europe resulting Ukraine invasion, she said. "Dependent weather conditions winter very likely may need start rationing energy very seriously avoid bigger energy issues December January when gas reserves start run low," she said.
Foley' calculations suggest households save £1.20 day £400 electricity bills if clocks put back end October, although exact amounts depend tariffs.
While Foley argues extension summer clock settings, there long debate whether actually scrap DST, introduced 1916 reduce energy demand during war prolonging evening daylight summer. Opponents argue causes sleep disturbance, contributes potential road accidents. originally proposed 1907 William Willett, builder great, great-grandfather Coldplay' Chris Martin, who well known song Clocks.
European parliament voted scrap hour change 2019, poll showed most EU citizens agreed. change yet implemented no longer applies UK after Brexit.
Foley did include gas savings or electricity gas commercial or industrial sectors her calculations, she said these would offer "even significant energy, cost emissions reductions", flattening evening peak energy demand 10%.
When comes concern road traffic collisions, Foley' research suggested most road deaths occur good visibility during day outside built- areas, usually Friday, Saturday, Sunday Monday, speed, tiredness alcohol main factors.
There would time zone issues between UK Ireland, creating two time zones between north south. Foley suggested remedied if two governments consulted emergency proposal abolish daylight saving.
government spokesperson said: " do agree claim, wrong suggest would save people money. current daylight-saving arrangements give optimal use available daylight across UK.
" know difficult time families across country, why put place immediate support coming winter. Government' Energy Price Guarantee reducing price energy millions most vulnerable households receiving £1,200 each additional support."
article amended 21 October 2022 add response government, received after publication.

escalating rollout gas heating, electricity cooking turning into "new coal" use worldwide must slashed nearly third decade avoid disastrous climate effects, according new report.
Gas often referred "bridge fuel" emits half carbon dioxide coal, many countries embraced while promising transition renewable energy order cut planet-heating emissions.
energy source, become easy cheap access due advance fracking extraction, still fossil fuel, new analysis finds now fastest-growing source carbon dioxide emissions, putting world risk blowing past dangerous global heating thresholds.
"Natural gas bridging fuel. fossil fuel," said Bill Hare, chief executive Climate Analytics lead author new report. "Gas new coal. Governments, investors financial sector must treat same way they do coal: phase out soon possible."
UK government, hosting crucial UN climate talks under way Glasgow, vowed help "consign coal history", summit producing agreement signed 40 countries, including major users Indonesia, Vietnam South Korea, phase out carbon-intensive fuel.
focus eliminating coal comes countries such US Australia ramped use supposedly cleaner sources gas sought cash global export market liquified gas new infrastructure ship around world.
growth gas had significant influence global heating, Climate Analytics report finding gas largest source carbon dioxide emissions increase past decade, rising 42% causing 60% methane emissions fossil fuel production. Methane short-lived potent greenhouse gas many times powerful CO2 trapping heat.
If world avert disastrous 1.5C global heating use gas should already decline, according report, projected cause 70% fossil CO2 emissions increase 2030 under current policies.
means unabated gas use must peak decade then drop sharply, analysis finds, necessitating decrease 30% below last year' levels 2030 then 65% decrease 2040. Renewable energy such solar wind should ramped take place gas, according report.
Hare said governments need questioned why they targeting gas well coal. "Ultimately, gas bridge nowhere," said.

" factually incorrect, politically very wrong," said Birol. " fact chances 1.5C narrowing, still achievable."

"I find emerging chorus unusual coalition people saying 1.5C dead factually politically wrong," told Guardian. "They jumping conclusions borne out data."
added claims "unhelpful" efforts shift global economy low-carbon footing. "They making mistake. Proponents existing energy systems beneficiaries if obituary 1.5C written," warned.
Investors financial institutions put off chorus claims 1.5C dead, added. "They react lower ambition," warned.
Birol pointed surge clean energy investment, wake Ukraine war soaring fossil fuel prices. added countries' current targets reaching net zero greenhouse gas emissions would result temperature rise 1.7C, if all pledges fulfilled, within striking distance 1.5C limit.
" factually wrong [ say 1.5C dead] evidence-based organisation," said Birol. "What I look numbers. say 1.5C dead never reach peak [ emissions] before 2030 dogmatic my view data-driven conclusion."
goal limiting global temperature rises 1.5C above pre-industrial levels, line repeated scientific advice, came under attack some countries two-week Cop27 summit Sharm el-Sheikh earlier month. final hours, attempts strengthen 1.5C limit denied, owing opposition Saudi Arabia other Gulf states, along, some points, China, Brazil few others. resolution phase down fossil fuels, proposed India backed least 80 countries, removed final text.
end, participants such UK EU, pushing stronger commitment 1.5C would require countries come forward firmer policies meet goal, said they "disappointed" result. Unfortunately, remains life support. all us need look ourselves mirror, consider if fully risen challenge past two weeks."
Birol acknowledged outcome summit weak 1.5C goal, said countries must still keep pushing.
"Looked point view energy, would accurate say global energy sector received strong signal Cop27," conceded. " absence such clear signal, message key actors may seem bit confused."
said economics transition clean energy clear, wind solar power now cheaper fossil fuels across much world, countries seeking expand clean energy sources matter national security industrial policy.
pointed global clean energy investments $1.3tn, said current policies, clean energy investments would reach $2tn 2030, would increase 50% beginning decade.
amount investment needs double again, however, stay within 1.5C. " extremely challenging, out reach," Birol said.

carbon capture proposal central Louisiana power plant titled "Project Diamond Vault" owner, Louisiana utility Cleco. utility says project "precious value" company, customers state.
Yet less six months after announcing project capture carbon plant' emissions store them underground near plant, Cleco revealed recent filing state regulator $900m carbon capture retrofit reduce electricity produced customers 30%.
Cleco maintains hasn't committed path., if decides produce additional power necessary run carbon capture process, increase plant' water use 55%, according studies similar power plants.
Louisiana project outlier.
Operating enough carbon capture keep climate crisis check would double humanity' water use, according University California, Berkeley researchers. Regardless method being used - power plant or capturing carbon directly air - power water needed.
Cleco proposal provides object lesson how one solution can exacerbate another problem.
Carbon capture equipment increases energy use, means water needed cool equipment. Photograph: Nicole Kelner/Floodlight
"These technologies mitigate climate change unintended environmental impacts, like water use water scarcity," said Lorenzo Rosa, principal investigator Carnegie Institution Science Stanford. Carbon capture sequestration increases water withdrawals power plants between 25% 200%, according Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change report cites Rosa' work.
same IPCC report says carbon capture help reduce fossil fuel pollution heating planet' climate causing extreme weather.
power generation unit Cleco plans add carbon capture technology - Madison 3 unit - makes electricity burning 70% petroleum coke, byproduct oil refining, 30% coal. It' estimated retrofitting coal-fired power plant carbon capture technology increase plant' water use 55%. similar increase expected retrofitting petcoke power plant, Rosa said.
Cleco, however, told Floodlight can't definitively say how much power electricity carbon capture process use until initial designs studies complete 2024. "Cleco committed research engineering highest level cannot speculate findings study," company said emailed response.
proposed carbon capture technology plant chemically strips carbon exhaust fossil fuel-burning facilities. Cleco expects running carbon capture equipment use 200 megawatts electricity, according recent Louisiana Public Service Commission filing. That' third 641-megawatt power station' current capacity.
industry says retrofitting certain fossil fuel-burning power plants cheaper shutting down facilities building renewable energy. offset cost technology, companies can use carbon force oil out ageing oilfields.
Adding technology power plants expensive. Wyoming, produces 40% nation' coal, passed law requiring electric utilities produce some power coal-burning power plants carbon capture. So far, that' proved unfeasible power companies warned technology would probably increase water use increase residential electricity bills much $100 month. study Energy Innovation: Policy Technology found coal plants retrofitted carbon capture technology three times expensive wind power twice expensive solar.

dozen carbon capture projects proposed Louisiana lawmakers industry leaders pitch carbon capture solution state' industrial carbon emissions. Last year' Bipartisan Infrastructure Law allocated $12bn federal funding toward carbon capture storage technologies, August' Inflation Reduction Act creates tax breaks power plants industrial facilities pursue carbon capture storage (CCS). federal support technology critical: no industrial-scale power plant US currently uses CCS, largely because costly, according Congressional Research Service.

Returning coal-fired power generation some parts Europe prevented strong progress cutting greenhouse gas emissions, data shows.
Emissions November EU lowest least 30 years, gas consumption, carbon power sector, power generation fossil fuels, according Centre Research Energy Clean Air.
month' freeze may result coal gas being burned December, after unusually mild November. Germany missed targets cutting consumption gas under cold conditions, German grid agency said.
only small proportion last month' drop fossil fuel use should attributed weather, according Centre Research Energy Clean Air analysis. mild temperatures contributed 6% reduction gas demand outside power sector - mostly heating - while actual demand fell 26%. Within power sector, milder temperatures can account two percentage points 12% drop demand, analysts found.
Lauri Myllyvirta, lead analyst author report, said data showed accusations against EU falling back climate commitments wrong. "There very widespread perception Europe going backwards climate change, because Ukraine war," said. "There frequent remarks effect Cop27, saying Europe going back coal. showing case - there misreading coal consumption."
Some member states, including Germany Poland, sought limited return burning coal power generation face soaring gas prices supply constraints after Russia' invasion Ukraine. UK put coal-fired power plants standby.
November, EU whole used less coal same month last, same month past three decades. Germany Poland both used less coal ago, though Finland slightly increased coal use.
There dramatic reductions output nuclear reactors Germany France. Germany made increase wind solar generation, while France reduced power demand substantially.
However, month' cold spell, temperatures 5C lower usual time, snow icy conditions across much northern Europe, stall progress cutting fossil fuel use winter.
Myllyvirta said: "Everything conditional weather. If big cold spell, see gas burning."
said early signs December trend lower emissions holding. first half December had colder weather before, added, total emissions remained well below 2021 levels, showing reduction gas electricity use mainly because weather.
However, power sector emissions started increasing again December. Myllyvirta said sector continued plagued poor performance nuclear, wind conditions poor, reduced gas use outside power sector kept emissions falling overall.
added transformation Europe' energy showed underlying trend strongly away fossil fuels. "If anyone had said ago Europe nearly eliminate reliance Russian fossil fuels 10 months, they would taken complete lunatic," said. " come quite close doing so, quite remarkable."
added governments should seek protect most vulnerable citizens dangerous effects energy price rises forced such sudden change. Europe go further weaning itself off Russian energy, fossil fuels general, should managed equitably, said.
"It' unfortunate so much reduction [ fossil fuel use] happened through high prices, having major social economic impacts," Myllyvirta said.

first---kind climate risks scorecard ranks Carlyle, Warburg Pincus KKR worst offenders among eight major private equity companies significant fossil fuel portfolios.
All three continue investing heavily greenhouse-gas-emitting projects no adequate plan transitioning away oil gas, according analysis two financial watchdog non-profits publicly available information. firms scant transparency political climate lobbying, report finds.
Private equity refers opaque form financing away public markets funds investors buy restructure companies including startups, troubled businesses real estate operations.
eight firms scorecard manage combined $3.6tn assets including $216bn energy projects - amount equivalent fossil fuel financing world' five biggest banks last.
Carlyle rated F, lowest climate credentials scorecard created Private Equity Stakeholder Project (Pesp) Americans Financial Reform Education Fund (Afref).
three-quarters Carlyle' energy investments fossil fuels, just 60% 2022 first half profits came through subsidiary NGP Energy Capital, focuses almost exclusively oil gas projects.

" scorecard provides important information analysis can help investors communities understand what these firms doing, makes very clear firm' climate commitments largely empty words, said Oscar Valdes Viera, research manager Afref co-author climate risks scorecard.
Globally, private equity manages trillions dollars wealthy individuals institutional investors such mutual funds, endowments pension funds. industry invested estimated $1tn energy sector since 2010, while there' growth renewables, lion' share still oil, gas coal.
Yet unlike banks other publicly listed companies, private equity firms exempt most financial disclosure rules, making extremely difficult track assets - or risks. means ordinary workers like firefighters, nurses teachers - whose pension funds invested private equity funds - little way knowing if retirement nest egg tied fossil fuels, scientists warn must phased out limit extent global heating.
Last week federal reserve announced plans require banks assess impact climate crisis energy transition long-term financial position, US central bank no power private equity firms.
Earlier, Guardian published snapshot industry' involvement some country' most controversial fossil fuel investments. climate risk scorecard digs deeper into risks surrounding dirty investments ranks worst offenders against each other.
Researchers assessed climate credentials Carlyle, Blackstone, Warburg Pincus, KKR, Ares, Brookfield/Oaktree, Apollo TPG using publicly available information. scorecard takes into account several transparency measures including disclosure emissions, political lobbying energy transition plans - information investors need make informed risk assessments.
Asset managers typically hold companies five years, so they divest fossil fuels decade, yet only one firm, Brookfield/Oaktree (D rating) reported even partial plan transitioning portfolio away polluting energy sources, report found. company' stated ambitions include achieving net zero emissions 2050, after reducing them two-thirds 2030 across one-third assets. Currently, fossil fuels comprise 53% Brookfield' energy portfolio. (Oaktree declined provide information researchers.)
"Private equity firms created large climate risks investors providing capital, especially they act fiduciaries public sector workers' retirement savings. societal sentiment grows support clean energy economy, risk doubling down dirty energy assets becoming clear," said Riddhi Mehta-Neugebauer, Pesp climate research director.

best performer TPG, scored B rating thanks relatively small number fossil fuel investments.

Hanover north-west Germany Wednesday became first large city announce energy-saving measures, including turning off hot water showers bathrooms city-run buildings leisure centres.

EU energy ministers clashed plan put price cap Russian gas, casting doubt whether measure go ahead.
Speaking after emergency talks Brussels response surging gas electricity prices, EU' energy commissioner, Kadri Simson, said "nothing decided" proposals curb Russia' income.
Vladimir Putin dismissed idea "stupid" threatened make Europe "freeze" winter, if cap agreed. Russia already slashed supplies Europe said resume flows previous volumes until EU lifts sanctions. Russian supply makes only 9% EU gas imports, down 40% before invasion Ukraine.
Simson defended cap plan reasonable. " context measure Russia gaining huge profits manipulating limiting, artificially, supply drive prices. cap would reduce these profits," she told reporters Friday.
27 ministers began meeting holding one-minute silence mark death Queen Elizabeth II, while EU flags outside Brussels institutions lowered tribute half-mast.
There consensus proposal cap high price EU-produced electricity renewable sources, such wind, solar nuclear, reduce energy consumption across region. European Commission favours mandatory 5% cut electricity use during peak hours, according leaked paper seen Guardian.
talks set stage period intense negotiations, European Commission president Ursula von der Leyen expected set out legal proposals EU' energy crisis response next Wednesday.
Countries import large volumes Russia, including Hungary, Slovakia Austria, spoken out against cap proposal because they fear Kremlin would halt all gas flows, plunging countries into recession.
"If price restrictions imposed exclusively Russian gas, would evidently lead immediate cut-off Russian gas supplies," said Hungarian foreign minister Péter Szijjártó, who attending meeting. " does take Nobel prize recognise."
dozen countries, including France Poland, say price cap should apply all imported gas, including liquified natural gas. EU energy commissioner voiced doubts approach, saying general price cap " present security supply challenge".
Since invasion Ukraine, EU scrambling secure supplies shipped other countries, such Qatar, Norway US, faces stiff competition Asia. "Right now important can replace decreasing Russian volumes alternative suppliers," Simson said.
Only Baltic states, long argued sanctions Russian gas, gave full-throated support plan. Riina Sikkut, Estonia' minister economic affairs infrastructure, urged other members ignore Putin' threats, saying: " blackmail, war waged outside Ukraine... political make Ukraine win."
Ministers aligned dealing distortion energy market, seen renewable nuclear energy companies reaping huge profits because price all electricity pegged price wholesale gas. proposals cap power wind, solar nuclear, redistribution revenues vulnerable consumers businesses.
Ministers backed plan reduce demand electricity, although Czech industry minister Jozef íkela, who chaired meeting, indicated member states wanted voluntary targets, rather legally binding obligation.
"EU energy ministers agreed EU needs comprehensive plan face ongoing energy crisis," said Simone Tagliapietra, senior energy expert Bruegel think tank. " all these measures extraordinarily complex engineered, take great political commitment member states quickly adopt them coming weeks. Europe off grand bargain energy."

would lead emissions equivalent current annual emissions African continent.

UK government launched investigation into renewable electricity deals amid growing concern extent "greenwashing" large energy firms claiming offer environmental benefits customers.
crackdown increasing numbers people switch renewable energy deal, government said would review how sector markets green electricity tariffs consumers.
Warning planned tighten rules stop firms exaggerating environmental benefits green electricity tariffs - marketing tactic known greenwashing - business department said investigation would focus whether labels such "100% renewable" or "green" remained fit purpose.
many nine million British households green tariffs, half all new deals launched energy providers now claimed come environmental benefits.
However, firms currently able advertise tariffs "green" even if some energy they supply comes fossil fuels, industry figures warned risks misleading consumers.
Suppliers can use several ways achieve green status under current rules, including through committing use 100% income customers invest developing renewable energy or striking deal existing windfarm or solar array buy electricity they produce.
Under government scheme, energy firms buy certificates known Renewable Energy Guarantees Origin, designed show consumers how much electricity they sell acquired clean sources.
When supplier claims 100% renewable energy tariff backed way, means match each megawatt hour electricity customers use certificates representing same amount renewable energy.
However, experts warned system open loopholes risk "double counting" UK' renewable energy supply use or even claiming foreign renewables own.
government said considering whether reform Rego system make "smarter". Energy suppliers forced provide clearer information consumers green tariffs, including type renewable energy used, such wind or solar, where when renewable power generated.
Anne-Marie Trevelyan, energy minister, said there concerns some energy companies overstating how environmentally friendly products.
"Millions UK households choosing make green switch, our energy comes renewables. I want people know when they sign green tariff, they investing companies make conscious choice invest renewable energy. Part ensuring companies being transparent possible where power comes," she said.
business department said polling YouGov showed almost two-thirds (62%) UK energy consumers said purchasing decisions influenced how eco-friendly energy tariff. However, 75% believed suppliers should open transparent tariffs, including how much renewable energy they buy other companies.
company Good Energy one those vocal critic greenwash tariffs. April said energy suppliers had some time able "mislead" customers who trying do right thing choosing green.
government publishing separate call evidence "third-party intermediaries" retail energy market, such price comparison websites, auto-switching services other brokers. moment, these operate outside retail market rules, ministers ask views whether regulatory framework needed.
Consumer protection advocates price comparison services called greater transparency around suppliers who market tariffs green.
Richard Neudegg, head regulation Uswitch.com, comparison service, said: " people purchasing green tariffs it' difficult bill payers know exactly what' under hood these deals. support any measures aim demystify green tariffs households."


cost living crisis hitting millions households get whole lot worse.
Gas bedrock power generation UK, supplying millions homes directly accounting 45% electricity supply. rocketed price, 400% past 1,000% since 2019, according ICE futures market.
prospect tripling energy price cap October £3,600 - £1,254 2019 - lies behind Bank England' warning long recession autumn, characterised soaring inflation, falling living standards rising unemployment.
central bank' response raise interest rates bring down inflation. Liz Truss, ahead race Tory leader prime minister, remedy - wide-ranging tax cuts spur growth. Labour would provide targeted support those struggling most bills, coupled measures increase investment.
there calls done. Here discuss some options.
lower price cap
What if regulator Ofgem said bills going October? half current 9.4% inflation rate can accounted energy cost increases, so effect would cap further price rises. lower inflation rate would take pressure off workers bargain higher wages, without strong wages, Bank would less need increase interest rates.
23 million households domestic energy bill governed energy price cap. Ofgem says if kept average dual-fuel tariff April' £1,971, then rising cost wholesale gas would swallowed industry.
regulator decided against route because says forcing utility companies absorb costs would make them all loss making force several into bankruptcy. They would need government subsidy billions pounds rescue loans those went bust.
government go down route become owner merged utility company. France already owns most national electricity supplier, EDF, buy remaining shares keep prices capped. Inflation France 5.8%.
bigger windfall tax
Britain produces gas oil North Sea. chancellor, Rishi Sunak agreed apply extra 25% levy industry' windfall profits said would raise £5bn.
address his previous concerns windfall tax would stymie investment, allowed firms offset 80% new investment costs against tax. Combined existing tax breaks, oil gas firms get 91p off corporation tax every pound spent investment.
there no evidence windfall profits being invested. industry plans investment 10 15 years ahead, windfalls usually handed shareholders higher dividends share buybacks. same case now. So government apply 25% without any tax breaks, generating sum nearer £15bn.
generous benefits
government' energy bills support scheme provides £400 discount bills October every household, £650 means-tested one-off payment eight million low-income households, £150 those disability benefits £300 pensioners. designed when forecast October price cap £2,800.
MPs business, energy industrial strategy committee warn package '"eclipsed scale crisis".
National Institute Economic Social Research said "any fiscal loosening would better directed universal credit", said should rise £25 week least six months October. addition, energy grant should rise £400 £600 11 million low-income households.
Bills so high subsidies would seen inflationary - just keeping wolf door.
Capping wages City
Bank officials said they increased interest rates 0.5 percentage points - largest margin 25 years - because they feared wage rises next would outstrip inflation generate wage/price spiral.
They admit there little evidence workers using muscle secure high wage rises moment, change if inflation remains high.
emphasise how weak pay demands so far, latest official figures show average earnings increased 4.3% June 6.2% bonuses. vast bulk bonuses paid financial, professional business services industries.
If Bank so worried, cap bonuses City firms regulates. would bring down average wages halt most egregious example pay growth, City firms immune energy crisis awarding themselves huge payouts.
other sectors where pay spiralled badly affected staff shortages, mainly due visa restrictions imposed UK EU workers. Higher wages welcome hospitality, travel leisure, firms trapped lack skilled replacements when staff move higher pay rival, forcing them turn down customers. Scrapping visa rules allowing free movement would another way ease pay pressures.

Rosalina Marrero spends best part each day ironing watching telenovelas her modest bungalow Puerto Rico' coastal Guayama province. When gets too hot or her asthma plays due toxic coal ash nearby power plant, 78--old widow rests adjustable hospital bed, clicks fan thanks God solar panels her roof.
Earlier, Marrero among two dozen residents low-income, predominantly Black neighbourhood blighted coal pollution, fitted rooftop solar storage system. Campaigners say systems like hers should rolled out widely tackle island' energy crisis global climate emergency - both exacerbating racialized health inequalities.
situation electricity dire.
Rooftop solar supply all Puerto Rico' energy needs
Ruth Santiago
Puerto Ricans can pay twice much electricity Americans mainland, depending sector they, yet earlier month power cuts hundreds thousands homes businesses caused widespread anger misery. Traffic lights failed, hospitals used expensive diesel generators keep dialysis machines ventilators running, fires broke out due fluctuations voltage.
Despite abysmal service, electricity rates increased four times so far.
hot humid Guayama, lights went out several hours almost every day those unable afford backup generator. thanks rooftop solar system, Marrero stay cool watch TV without worrying food going off fridge.
"It' best thing that' happened me. I thank God because my poor neighbours suffering I've always got electricity. I feel secure, if get another big hurricane like Maria, I won't suffer so much," said Marrero.
Marrero lucky because while Puerto Rico gets enough sunlight meet residential electricity needs least four times, less 3% island' energy comes renewable sources. rest generated imported fossil fuels: 49% petroleum 29% natural gas, while coal accounts 19%.
Hurricane Maria wake- call: only exposed Puerto Rico' vulnerability extreme weather events linked global heating, focused attention economic environmental ramifications island' dependence fossil fuels local communities greenhouse gas emissions.
gave rise social movement came plan called Queremos Sol, or want sun - evidence-based roadmap make Puerto Rico' energy system self-sufficient swapping fossil fuels onsite small solar grids distributed throughout island.
concept simple: localized system doesn't require moving electricity centralized power plants along overhead wires local substations would better equipped withstand recover super storms other natural disasters. Onsite batteries would better cover critical needs during disruptions.
Experts say such radical change necessary comply Puerto Rico' ambitious energy policy, mandates 40% electricity must come renewable resources 2025 100% 2050.
would help US comply Paris agreement, Biden rejoined first day his presidency, well administration' commitment achieve net zero emissions investing green infrastructure jobs address climate change environmental justice.
other hand, local authorities energy sector insist electricity must generated avoid shortages power cuts - claims campaigners dismiss ruse justify further investment gas transmission infrastructure, official documents suggest there' enough capacity cover demand.
What' agreed Puerto Rico pivotal point once there' real money table: last September, Federal Emergency Management Agency (Fema) approved unprecedented $9.6bn reconstruct island' energy system currently deciding how money should spent.
"Rooftop solar supply all Puerto Rico' energy needs. It' viable, sustainable reliable current centralized system energy industry authorities want rebuild," said Ruth Santiago, lawyer environmental policy expert who contributed Queremos Sol. " need Biden administration act. critical time, lot stake."
move renewables - how fast?
Puerto Rico tropical Caribbean archipelago US territory located thousand miles or so south-east Miami. main island mostly mountains surrounded narrow coastal plains where majority 3 million habitants reside towns cities.
past two decades, Puerto Rico among three territories (along Haiti Myanmar) most affected extreme weather such storms, floods, heatwaves droughts, according Germanwatch Climate Risk Index.
September 2017 hurricanes Irma Maria made landfall two weeks apart destroyed much Puerto Rico' electricity transmission distribution infrastructure (including industrial wind farms), leading longest blackout US history.
3,000 people died aftermath homes, businesses healthcare facilities left without power months. Marrero cut off 15 weeks, recalls having buy just enough food last each day she couldn't afford generator fridge or fan.
addition increasingly frequent intense catastrophic weather events, global heating causing slower changes such rising sea levels ocean warming endanger island' infrastructure.
climate crisis isn't only threat facing Puerto Rico: 6.4 magnitude earthquake January 2020 left two-thirds residents without power, significantly damaged island' two gas-fired power plants.
fact, much existing energy infrastructure (plants, transmitter towers, poles cables) flood-prone areas or risk sea-level rise, storm surges tsunamis, well strong winds earthquake damage.
It' one reason why rooftop systems, industrial farms, proposed Queremos Sol - centralised renewable plants rely transmission distribution lines. Rooftop solar relies batteries local distribution networks, would easier fix if damaged. addition, Puerto Rico lacks open land, so large-scale wind solar farms would affect scarce agricultural lands ecologically sensitive areas.
It' widely agreed Puerto Rico' energy system disarray.
publicly owned utility (Prepa), like Puerto Rico, bankruptcy maintenance neglected years. latest spate power cuts led resignations top Prepa, responsible generating electricity, angry public protests against Luma, private US-Canadian consortium took transmission distribution June.
What' disputed how quickly island should move renewables.
Official documents show government Puerto Rico, Prepa Luma want spend most historic funds Federal Emergency Management Agency (Fema) rebuilding existing transmission system new gas-fired plants, only fraction left renewables.
Fema told Guardian Prepa Luma so far submitted 88 projects, detailed work plans would evaluated before final approval.
Fernando Gil, new chairman Prepa' board, told Guardian progress towards legally binding renewable targets had too slow, denied vested interests blame. " need stabilize system, generate power proactive maintenance while planning renewables, flexible... Gas way cleaner can efficient if combined other fuels."
Yet Tomá Torres, consumer representative board, said utility should investing rooftop solar installation now. "Puerto Rico proper laws, regulations funds achieve energy transformation, just executed."
Luma spokesperson said: " key measures including reliability, customer service safety, Puerto Rico worst electrical service North America. take time concerted effort set right."
battle Puerto Rico' energy future intensifies, many ordinary people it' matter life death.
Solar power 'can save lives'
Guayama, 72--old Margarita Velázquez diagnosed COPD, chronic respiratory condition, few years after nearby coal plant opened 2002. "I stopped being able breathe. I remember doctor asked me how long I'd smoking, I've never smoked!"
bad days, when coal ash hovers air community, Velázquez finds hard stop wheezing needs electric-powered nebulizer machine open her airways. anxiety losing power gone since she selected rooftop project.
coal plant Guayama. Photograph: Angel Valentin/ Guardian
"It' given me some peace mind," she said.
nearby plant, huge pile grey ash laden toxic heavy metals waiting shipped Florida Georgia disposal. When wind picks, ash scatters through air.
years hundreds thousands tonnes dumped landfills, roads fields, contaminating air drinking water sources mostly low-income neighborhoods.
coal plant cost lives. Public health researchers found higher rates several chronic conditions among communities near plant, including respiratory illness, heart disease, allergies skin complaints. incidence cancers increased 65% decade after plant opened, while numbers patients kidney failure requiring dialysis risen, according Dr Gerson Jiménez, director Guayama hospital where worked four decades.
Amid growing backlash, coal ash must now removed, huge toxic deposits remain scattered across island now others face same deadly consequences. "It' now being sent poor communities colour mainland, who can expect see increase same lethal diseases years come," said Jiménez.
Puerto Rico' energy reforms, passed 2019 bipartisan support, mandate phasing out coal 2028 ( company' 25- contract ends 2027), advocates argue rolling out rooftop solar make plant obsolete sooner therefore save lives.
José Vázquez, 76, Velázquez' husband member Guayama Community United Your Health - group campaigning plant' closure, cleanup compensation - said: "There big politicians big interests struggle. middle like ham sandwich."
Perhaps there another way.
Solar town
Adjuntas small mountain town surrounded lush forests central midwestern part island where temperate climate provides relief steamy coasts.
Here, solar power movement dates back two decades grassroots environmental group called Casa Pueblo, organized community kick out mining company before embarking crusade make town energy independent.
Since Maria, hundreds rooftop onsite solar systems installed homes, emergency services critical businesses around Adjuntas, known locally Pueblo Solar (Solar Town).
initiative relies locally trained volunteers, donations grants groups like US non-profit Honnold Foundation, includes vulnerable households, nursing home, fire station, community radio station selection general stores serve isolated neighbourhoods stranded after hurricanes.
town' picturesque main square, all 25 businesses - including pizzeria, pharmacy hardware store - created micro energy network social justice twist: savings generating sharing own energy help fund solar systems low-income residents who homebound or need keep medication refrigerated.
edge town, José Hernández runs minimart Maria partially destroyed left without power six months. hurricane triggered mass exodus mainland mostly working-age folks left unemployed or homeless, Hernández, 33, decided stay rebuild.
hard expand business frequent power cuts force him close store damage fridges, costly repair.
six months ago Hernández obtained grant solar panels, then used his savings buy other equipment. Since then, had uninterrupted power his electricity bill fallen drastically, allowing him hire employee. "It' given me sense tranquillity. It' exciting, I can now expand invest my future."

added: "Some those same developed countries introducing universal subsidies gas stations, while others reopening coal plants. difficult justify such steps even temporary basis."

secret court system allows fossil fuel investors sue governments vast amounts money accused institutional bias, self-regulation issues perceived conflicts interest, drumbeat EU countries leaving threatens turn into samba march.
Wednesday, EU meeting discuss reform energy charter treaty (ECT) end last week, Germany became latest European country announce intention leave treaty. Slovenia exited earlier week, after similar moves France, Netherlands, Spain Poland. UK now one last large economies remain ECT.
ECT' investment arbitration courts set 1998 protect energy firms operating former Soviet Union countries government expropriation regulation. They gave companies right seek compensation if legislation or policies enacted seen hostile, using investor-state dispute mechanism stirred much public controversy during negotiation Transatlantic Trade Investment Partnership (TTIP).
treaty since signed 52 countries across Europe central Asia, oil, gas coal firms being awarded $100bn ECT tribunals. UK oil firm Rockhopper recently awarded $190m case brought against Italy, contesting decision. countries sought curb emissions line Paris climate agreement after 2015, number claims being brought exploded. Renewables companies brought number cases.
signatories prepare crunch meeting Mongolia 22 November, ECT facing what one critic called "crisis legitimacy" several reasons, including how tribunals composed, feeding growing concerns perceived conflicts interest.
ECT' tribunals can proceed under several systems, World Bank' International Centre Settlement International Disputes (ICSID) rules most commonly used. system different most national legal systems, employ publicly appointed independent judiciary. ICSID' courts work independent arbitrators appointed parties, who may work different roles employers conflicting interests within system.
Each case decided panel three arbitrators: one appointed state, one investor, third who acts president or chair selected other two arbitrators. case then argued before panel lawyers acting counsel each party. Cases held behind closed doors, there no obligation release outcome. choice arbitrator can challenged if party disagrees, there no guarantee succeed.
"Double hatting" occurs when lawyer takes one role across different cases - example - sometimes acting counsel investor, other times acting president, who supposed adjudicate independently.
analysis cases brought so far under ECT - carried out Guardian, Transnational Institute Powershift - found significant number cases, individual who had previously acted arbitrator appointed investor one case appointed act counsel (or advocate) party another similar case.
Without full disclosure - one main reasons challenge arbitrator - there can no guarantee arbitrators had links law firm (or lawyers) acting counsel cases they adjudicating, or they acted counsel similar cases, leading risk them prejudging issues play. Indeed, reputations regard may lead some clients seek them out.
191 arbitrators nominated sit ECT hearings, just 37 elite lawyers - 18% pool - heard half all ECT' cases. Seventeen members group acted role arbitrator legal counsel, ECT other investment courts.
Sixteen 17 arbitrators sat fossil fuel cases - most chosen investors - only three 37 sat fossil fuel-related arbitration panel.
cases "double hatters" took part arbitrators, 58% won investors, according analysis.
Under ICSID rules, arbitrators should "persons high moral character recognised competence fields law, commerce, industry or finance, who may relied exercise independent judgment".
However, these rules self-regulated arbitrators - critics say they unenforceable - successful removal arbitrators rare. Spain, example, just one proposed ECT arbitrator blocked, out 19 challenges.
Many arbitrators believe they can separate different roles they may play different cases.
Klaus Sachs, ECT-focused arbitrator based Munich, said " every profession, excellence way success among those arbitrators who high demand, you very qualified people. They very good lawyers."
There, added, " lot new talent coming so market changing new generation enters field".
However, critics argue recent overstated trend. They say allowing parties appoint arbitrators who acted counsel similar cases opens what Lucí Bárcena, Transnational Institute, called " Pandora' box conflicts interest".
caused "long-running heated debate within international commercial arbitration community", according 2017 study., international legal expert Philippe Sands asked whether lawyer impartially wear hat arbitrator morning counsel afternoon. "Speaking myself, I find difficult imagine I do so," said.
George Kahale III, chair partner law firm Curtis, Mallet-Prevost, Colt & Mosle, represents states international arbitration cases, said small number arbitrators available states caused " clear structural bias" toward investors ECT' courts.
" pool arbitration candidates who I would consider 'straight shooters'... very, very small, whereas pool investor-friendly arbitrators wide Pacific Ocean," said, video call New York.
Kahale, ECT veteran, said had sat many ISDS cases where knew what outcome tribunal would soon saw composition.
"There' virtually no chance getting someone small pool [ arbitrators] states would normally consider appointing," said.
Defenders system say pool defending states filled "very prominent good arbitrators", "relatively large", albeit smaller investors.
"When comes chair, obviously becomes complicated," Sachs said. " I don't think, when party learns I've appointed chair case, they know what outcome, I feel true large majority real professional arbitrators."
Sachs stressed challenging arbitrators lawyerly tactic " most cases, decisions well-reasoned balanced". accepted arbitrators " often" removed under challenge.
Kahale said factoring all - what called " virtual explosion" size damages claims - result " crisis legitimacy within [ISDS] system". view shared Sands.
Laurence Tubiana, one architects Paris deal chief executive European Climate Foundation, said analysis "clearly shows energy charter treaty' court system reeks potential conflicts interests favour fossil fuel investors threaten Paris climate agreement. Once again, oil gas industry found ways control game."
Within system shower fossil fuel investors trillion dollars compensation pay-offs 2050, there widespread fears ECT spark what UN Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change (IPCC) called "regulatory chill" just moment new climate laws needed.
" energy charter treaty consistent Paris agreement," said Patrice Dreiski, former ECT executive. " main goal ECT promote protect fossil fuels investment, all goal Paris agreement."
date, investors won 64% concluded ECT cases, three-quarters covered fossil fuel sector, according analysis.
data compiled mostly Transnational Institute UN Conference Trade Development database, ECT' own overview cases, ICSID Permanent Court Arbitration databases administer disputes, public tribunal documents published italaw.com specialised media.
impression legal web geared structurally benefit fossil fuel investors may even reflected within ECT secretariat itself, promotes ECT conferences, oversees treaty rules, provides institutional support achieve ECT' objectives.
Almost three-quarters (72%) experts secretariat' industry advisory panel - provides policy advice body " particular focus risk mitigation improvement business climate" - work fossil fuel companies or financial beneficiaries.
Guy Lentz, secretary general ECT secretariat former Shell executive, accepted panel "structurally biased", added: " want many renewable energy companies. working really hard, takes time."
Fabian Flues, trade investment adviser Berlin-based non-profit Powershift, said analysis showed ECT "dominated interests stake maintaining highly lucrative system investment arbitration. enables them rake huge fees, often coming out taxpayers' pockets."
However, disquiet treaty' threat timely climate action now spurred Brussels propose phasing out ECT' writ within EU' border, even bloc' own members queue jettison pact.
appears, however, UK joining queue. government spokesperson said only signatories " decide whether adopt modernised energy charter treaty energy charter conference 22 November".

Australia' coal-fired power plants likely shut almost triple pace currently announced, Victoria' brown coal fleet closed just decade main electricity grid becoming coal-free 2043, according market operator.
draft Integrated System Plan 2022, industry blueprint updated every two years released Friday Australian Energy Market Operator, plots how grid serving eastern Australia change meet emissions reduction market goals.
detailed four scenarios based extensive consultation 18 months. most probable path, dubbed "Step Change" option, anticipates nine-fold increase large-scale renewable energy, such wind solar farms, electricity supplies double meet decarbonisation transport other sectors without coal.
Solar panels homes increase five times, firming capacity form batteries other storage triple 2050. 10,000km new transmission needed connect increasingly dispersed supply.
Owners some coal generators already accelerated declared retirement dates, based market, financial operating pressures caused rise renewable generation, report said.
" financial viability existing thermal generation become increasingly uncertain, particularly coal-fired generation less able adjust generation levels rapidly response changes market prices," said. "Significant financial decisions repair maintain plant may harder justify under uncertainty, potentially resulting declining plant reliability."
ISP, finalised next, comes major states such Victoria NSW develop renewable energy zones or promote renewables' penetration through auctions or offer financial support households install solar panels.

report notes transformation grid already "outpaced all expectations", Australia installing five times amount large-scale renewables 2019 per capita basis European Union.
" last two years, through pandemic, [such renewables] development accelerated, 40% [ ] now committed or anticipated connected grid 2023-24 forecast 2020 ISP, said.
grid' share renewables risen high 61% during periods September, Aemo now forecasts stints 100% early 2025.

2030, bioenergy expected account third "low-carbon" energy, according report International Energy Agency.
UK top importer wood pellets biomass, 2019 5m metric tonnes them brought US. Burning biomass important part UK' net zero strategy, subsidised £5.6bn past decade.
Cutting down trees bioenergy results release carbon would otherwise had locked carbon-rich forests. increases emissions creates "carbon debt", only paid off decades or even centuries later if trees regrown, scientists say.
Burning wood electricity inefficient, releasing comparatively carbon into atmosphere gas or coal. Additional energy used harvest transport wood. Experts warning years climate impacts bioenergy, now they finding out dire risks nature too, many cases protected forests being affected.

These all home irreplaceable rare plant species, mammals, migratory residential birds."
Rare species such prothonotary warbler, boreal woodland caribou black stork among those declining result forest degradation.

What relationship between Russia' threats Ukraine gas price crisis?
Gas prices soaring globally, largely because resurgence demand fossil fuels after Covid-19 pandemic lockdowns, depleted storage difficulty ramping supply after disruptions past two years. prices significantly affected Russia' recent decision tighten gas supplies Europe, experts believe driven Vladimir Putin' desire move against Ukraine.
Europe dependent Russia 40% natural gas supplies, despite expansion renewable energy past two decades, dependency increasing countries shift gas, away dirtier coal. Germany particularly vulnerable, shut down nearly all nuclear power stations aims eliminate coal 2030.
major new gas pipeline, Gazprom' $11bn Nord Stream 2 Russia across Baltic Germany, would heat 26m German homes, focus Germany' diplomatic relations Russia since announced 2015. Construction 1,200km pipeline completed last, no gas yet flowed future now doubt Germany' chancellor Olaf Scholz dramatically halted approval project Tuesday.
Russia' gas pipelines Europe
Concerns Russia using gas supplies weapon achieve political aims well founded. Fatih Birol, executive director International Energy Agency, noted Russia' decision drop gas supplies Europe quarter came heightening tension towards Ukraine. "I hope only coincidence," told Guardian.
really another war fossil fuels?
No. Energy resources focus threatened conflict. Vladimir Putin long history territorial ambitions former Soviet nations, made explicit week, attempts exert political control Ukraine. Putin said supporters concerned possibility Nato expansion, although many analysts say pretext.
So Ukraine crisis war resources, many implications resource use. Russia effectively weaponising dominance European gas supply political ends. Reducing reliance Russian gas urgent necessity EU reach net zero emissions, would diminish Putin' political leverage EU.
worth noting longer term, Europe weans itself off gas pursues net zero emissions, value political weapon wane rapidly. Russia' industries never recovered fall communism, economy now based overwhelmingly export fossil fuels, much rest made energy-dependent mineral resources, such iron, steel, aluminium other metals, some agriculture.
Four 10 roubles accruing Russia' federal budget pre-pandemic came oil gas, made 60% Russian exports 2019. Kremlin strategists therefore keenly aware longer term global move net zero threatens whole basis Russia' economy global influence.
Why Europe so dependent Russian gas?
Historically, much Europe heavily dependent coal power heating. Renewable energy generation doubled since 2004, reached 22% final energy production 2020.
However, same time share generated nuclear power fallen, third EU' electricity 1995 quarter today. After accident Japan' Fukushima nuclear power plant 2011, Germany' then chancellor Angela Merkel announced "Energiewende" - energy shift - would see all country' reactors shut down.
increased Germany' need gas, similar moves away coal towards lower emissions other countries - including UK, despite North Sea gas production net importer gas - heightened dependency.
geopolitical implications dependency, well climate impacts, now unignorable. Paul Bledsoe, former Clinton White House climate adviser, now Progressive Policy Institute Washington DC, says: " taken current crisis Germany EU recognise co-dependence Russian gas geopolitical climate nightmare they must finally awake. Reducing Russian gas reliance huge climate moral imperative Europe must prioritise."

would momentous move Russia. Any further reductions Russian gas supply Europe would severe consequences, Europe Russia itself.
